{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/stats.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../src/types/transcript.js'\nimport {\n  computeStats,\n  buildDayBuckets,\n  buildWeekBuckets,\n  extractToolNames,\n  accumulateToolCounts,\n} from '../../src/report/stats.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {\n  return {\n    client: 'codex',\n    project: 'test',\n    session_id: 'abc-123',\n    raw_sha256: 'deadbeef',\n    title: 'Test Session',\n    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',\n    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',\n    cwd: '/home/user',\n    branch: 'main',\n    raw_source_path: '/src/file.jsonl',\n    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',\n    global_raw_path: '',\n    global_clean_path: '',\n    global_manifest_path: '',\n    repo_raw_path: '',\n    repo_clean_path: '',\n    repo_manifest_path: '',\n    message_count: 10,\n    tool_event_count: 5,\n    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',\n    repo_mirror_enabled: true,\n    ...overrides,\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// computeStats\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('computeStats', () => {\n  it('handles zero manifests', () => {\n    const stats = computeStats([])\n    expect(stats.totalSessions).toBe(0)\n    expect(stats.totalMessages).toBe(0)\n    expect(stats.totalToolEvents).toBe(0)\n    expect(stats.activeDays).toBe(0)\n    expect(stats.dateRange.from).toBe('')\n    expect(stats.dateRange.to).toBe('')\n    expect(stats.clientCounts).toEqual({})\n    expect(stats.dayBuckets).toEqual({})\n    expect(stats.weekBuckets).toEqual({})\n    expect(stats.toolCounts).toEqual({})\n  })\n\n  it('sums messages and tool events', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ message_count: 10, tool_event_count: 5 })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'xyz', message_count: 20, tool_event_count: 3 })\n    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2])\n    expect(stats.totalSessions).toBe(2)\n    expect(stats.totalMessages).toBe(30)\n    expect(stats.totalToolEvents).toBe(8)\n  })\n\n  it('counts client distribution', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ client: 'codex' })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', client: 'claude' })\n    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', client: 'claude' })\n    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2, m3])\n    expect(stats.clientCounts['codex']).toBe(1)\n    expect(stats.clientCounts['claude']).toBe(2)\n  })\n\n  it('counts active days correctly', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-10T14:00:00Z' }) // same day\n    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', started_at: '2026-03-11T08:00:00Z' }) // different day\n    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2, m3])\n    expect(stats.activeDays).toBe(2)\n  })\n\n  it('computes date range', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-15T00:00:00Z' })\n    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2])\n    expect(stats.dateRange.from).toBe('2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')\n    expect(stats.dateRange.to).toBe('2026-03-15T00:00:00Z')\n  })\n\n  it('accepts external toolCounts', () => {\n    const stats = computeStats([], { Bash: 10, Read: 5 })\n    expect(stats.toolCounts['Bash']).toBe(10)\n    expect(stats.toolCounts['Read']).toBe(5)\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildDayBuckets\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildDayBuckets', () => {\n  it('returns empty for zero manifests', () => {\n    expect(buildDayBuckets([])).toEqual({})\n  })\n\n  it('buckets sessions by date', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-10T14:00:00Z' })\n    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', started_at: '2026-03-11T08:00:00Z' })\n    const buckets = buildDayBuckets([m1, m2, m3])\n    expect(buckets['2026-03-10']).toBe(2)\n    expect(buckets['2026-03-11']).toBe(1)\n  })\n\n  it('ignores manifests with empty started_at', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest({ started_at: '' })\n    expect(buildDayBuckets([m])).toEqual({})\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildWeekBuckets\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildWeekBuckets', () => {\n  it('returns empty for zero manifests', () => {\n    expect(buildWeekBuckets([])).toEqual({})\n  })\n\n  it('buckets messages by ISO week', () => {\n    // 2026-03-10 is in week 2026-W11\n    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z', message_count: 5 })\n    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-11T10:00:00Z', message_count: 3 })\n    const buckets = buildWeekBuckets([m1, m2])\n    // Both are in the same week\n    const weekKey = Object.keys(buckets)[0]\n    expect(weekKey).toMatch(/^\\d{4}-W\\d{2}$/)\n    const val = weekKey ? buckets[weekKey] : 0\n    expect(val).toBe(8)\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// extractToolNames\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('extractToolNames', () => {\n  it('extracts first word as tool name', () => {\n    const names = extractToolNames(['Bash input={\"command\":\"ls\"}', 'Read /file.ts'])\n    expect(names[0]).toBe('Bash')\n    expect(names[1]).toBe('Read')\n  })\n\n  it('handles underscore and hyphen in names', () => {\n    const names = extractToolNames(['function_call input=...', 'custom-tool arg=1'])\n    expect(names[0]).toBe('function_call')\n    expect(names[1]).toBe('custom-tool')\n  })\n\n  it('returns unknown for non-identifier start', () => {\n    const names = extractToolNames(['123abc', '!cmd'])\n    expect(names[0]).toBe('unknown')\n    expect(names[1]).toBe('unknown')\n  })\n\n  it('handles empty array', () => {\n    expect(extractToolNames([])).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('handles empty string entries', () => {\n    const names = extractToolNames([''])\n    expect(names[0]).toBe('unknown')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// accumulateToolCounts\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('accumulateToolCounts', () => {\n  it('accumulates counts into new map', () => {\n    const base = { Bash: 3 }\n    const result = accumulateToolCounts(base, ['Bash', 'Read', 'Bash'])\n    expect(result['Bash']).toBe(5)\n    expect(result['Read']).toBe(1)\n  })\n\n  it('does not mutate input', () => {\n    const base = { Bash: 1 }\n    accumulateToolCounts(base, ['Bash'])\n    expect(base['Bash']).toBe(1)\n  })\n})\n"
}

  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/charts.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'\nimport {\n  renderHeatmap,\n  renderClientDoughnut,\n  renderWeeklyLine,\n  renderToolBar,\n} from '../../../src/report/render/charts.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderHeatmap\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('renderHeatmap', () => {\n  it('produces valid SVG with empty data', () => {\n    const svg = renderHeatmap({})\n    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')\n    expect(svg).toContain('</svg>')\n    expect(svg).toContain('<rect')\n  })\n\n  it('applies green color for active days', () => {\n    const today = new Date()\n    const dateStr = today.toISOString().slice(0, 10)\n    const svg = renderHeatmap({ [dateStr]: 5 })\n    // Should have a non-default color (not #161b22) for an active day\n    expect(svg).toContain('#26a641')\n  })\n\n  it('includes day count in title tooltip', () => {\n    const today = new Date()\n    const dateStr = today.toISOString().slice(0, 10)\n    const svg = renderHeatmap({ [dateStr]: 3 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('3 session')\n  })\n\n  it('renders heatmap legend', () => {\n    const svg = renderHeatmap({})\n    expect(svg).toContain('Less')\n    expect(svg).toContain('More')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderClientDoughnut\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('renderClientDoughnut', () => {\n  it('renders empty state gracefully (all-zero)', () => {\n    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({})\n    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')\n    expect(svg).toContain('No data')\n  })\n\n  it('renders segments for each client', () => {\n    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 5, claude: 3, gemini: 2 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')\n    expect(svg).toContain('codex')\n    expect(svg).toContain('claude')\n    expect(svg).toContain('gemini')\n  })\n\n  it('shows total session count in center', () => {\n    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 7, claude: 3 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('10')\n    expect(svg).toContain('sessions')\n  })\n\n  it('handles single client', () => {\n    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 10 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('10')\n    expect(svg).not.toContain('No data')\n  })\n\n  it('skips zero-count clients', () => {\n    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 5, claude: 0 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('codex')\n    expect(svg).not.toContain('>claude<')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderWeeklyLine\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('renderWeeklyLine', () => {\n  it('produces valid SVG with empty data', () => {\n    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({})\n    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')\n    expect(svg).toContain('<polyline')\n  })\n\n  it('renders area path and line', () => {\n    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({ '2026-W10': 10, '2026-W11': 20 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('<path')\n    expect(svg).toContain('<polyline')\n  })\n\n  it('handles single non-zero week', () => {\n    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({ '2026-W01': 5 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')\n    expect(svg).not.toContain('NaN')\n  })\n\n  it('does not contain NaN with all-zero data', () => {\n    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({})\n    expect(svg).not.toContain('NaN')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderToolBar\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('renderToolBar', () => {\n  it('shows no-data message for empty input', () => {\n    const svg = renderToolBar({})\n    expect(svg).toContain('No tool data available')\n  })\n\n  it('renders bars for provided tools', () => {\n    const svg = renderToolBar({ Bash: 10, Read: 5, Write: 3 })\n    expect(svg).toContain('Bash')\n    expect(svg).toContain('Read')\n    expect(svg).toContain('Write')\n  })\n\n  it('limits to top 10 tools', () => {\n    const tools: Record<string, number> = {}\n    for (let i = 0; i < 15; i++) {\n      tools[`Tool${i}`] = 15 - i\n    }\n    const svg = renderToolBar(tools)\n    // Should not contain Tool10 (11th ranked) since sorted by count desc\n    const rectCount = (svg.match(/<rect/g) ?? []).length\n    expect(rectCount).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10)\n  })\n\n  it('sorts tools by count descending', () => {\n    const svg = renderToolBar({ LowUsage: 1, HighUsage: 100 })\n    const highPos = svg.indexOf('HighUsage')\n    const lowPos = svg.indexOf('LowUsage')\n    expect(highPos).toBeGreaterThan(-1)\n    expect(lowPos).toBeGreaterThan(-1)\n    // HighUsage should appear before LowUsage in the SVG\n    expect(highPos).toBeLessThan(lowPos)\n  })\n})\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/links.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'\nimport { extractLinks, buildLinkGraph } from '../../../src/report/render/links.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nlet tmpDir: string\n\nbeforeEach(() => {\n  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'links-test-'))\n})\n\nafterEach(() => {\n  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n})\n\nfunction makeManifest(id: string, stem: string, content?: string): ManifestEntry {\n  const cleanRelPath = `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`\n  if (content !== undefined) {\n    const fullPath = join(tmpDir, cleanRelPath)\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(fullPath, content, 'utf-8')\n  }\n  return {\n    client: 'codex',\n    project: 'test',\n    session_id: id,\n    raw_sha256: id.slice(0, 8).padEnd(64, '0'),\n    title: `Session ${id}`,\n    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',\n    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',\n    cwd: '',\n    branch: 'main',\n    raw_source_path: '',\n    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',\n    global_raw_path: '',\n    global_clean_path: '',\n    global_manifest_path: '',\n    repo_raw_path: '',\n    repo_clean_path: cleanRelPath,\n    repo_manifest_path: '',\n    message_count: 1,\n    tool_event_count: 0,\n    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',\n    repo_mirror_enabled: true,\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// extractLinks\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('extractLinks', () => {\n  it('detects [[id]] references', () => {\n    const refs = extractLinks('See [[session-abc]] for details.')\n    expect(refs).toContain('session-abc')\n  })\n\n  it('detects multiple references', () => {\n    const refs = extractLinks('[[a]] and [[b]] are related.')\n    expect(refs).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(refs).toContain('a')\n    expect(refs).toContain('b')\n  })\n\n  it('ignores partial brackets: single bracket', () => {\n    const refs = extractLinks('[not a link] and [another')\n    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)\n  })\n\n  it('ignores newlines inside brackets', () => {\n    const refs = extractLinks('[[line1\\nline2]]')\n    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)\n  })\n\n  it('trims whitespace from refs', () => {\n    const refs = extractLinks('[[ spaced ]]')\n    expect(refs[0]).toBe('spaced')\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty for text with no links', () => {\n    expect(extractLinks('No links here.')).toEqual([])\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildLinkGraph\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildLinkGraph', () => {\n  it('builds forward and back links', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\\n### 1. user\\nSee [[bbb]]')\n    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '## Messages\\n### 1. user\\nNo links here')\n\n    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)\n    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')\n    expect(graph.backlinks['bbb']).toContain('aaa')\n  })\n\n  it('ignores self-references', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\\n### 1. user\\nSee [[aaa]]')\n\n    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1], tmpDir)\n    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)\n    expect(graph.backlinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)\n  })\n\n  it('handles manifests with no clean file', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest('zzz', 'zzz__00000000') // no content written\n    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m], tmpDir)\n    expect(graph.forwardLinks).toEqual({})\n    expect(graph.backlinks).toEqual({})\n  })\n\n  it('deduplicates links', () => {\n    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\\n### 1. user\\n[[bbb]] [[bbb]] again')\n    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '')\n\n    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)\n    const fwd = graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []\n    const count = fwd.filter(x => x === 'bbb').length\n    expect(count).toBe(1)\n  })\n\n  it('matches by stem as well as session_id', () => {\n    // The link text is the stem of the clean file\n    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\\n### 1. user\\nSee [[bbb__11111111]]')\n    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__11111111', '')\n\n    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)\n    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')\n  })\n})\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/search.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'\nimport { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from '../../../src/report/render/search.js'\nimport type { SearchIndexEntry } from '../../../src/types/report.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction makeManifest(id: string, client = 'codex', repoClean = ''): ManifestEntry {\n  return {\n    client,\n    project: 'test',\n    session_id: id,\n    raw_sha256: id.padEnd(64, '0'),\n    title: `Session ${id}`,\n    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',\n    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',\n    cwd: '',\n    branch: 'main',\n    raw_source_path: '',\n    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',\n    global_raw_path: '',\n    global_clean_path: '',\n    global_manifest_path: '',\n    repo_raw_path: '',\n    repo_clean_path: repoClean,\n    repo_manifest_path: '',\n    message_count: 5,\n    tool_event_count: 2,\n    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',\n    repo_mirror_enabled: true,\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildSearchIndex\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildSearchIndex', () => {\n  it('builds index with correct fields', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest('abc', 'codex', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/abc__deadbeef.md')\n    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => 'some snippet')\n    expect(index).toHaveLength(1)\n    const entry = index[0]!\n    expect(entry.title).toBe('Session abc')\n    expect(entry.client).toBe('codex')\n    expect(entry.date).toBe('2026-03-10')\n    expect(entry.snippet).toBe('some snippet')\n  })\n\n  it('uses session_id as stem when no clean path', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest('myid', 'claude', '')\n    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')\n    expect(index[0]!.url).toContain('myid')\n  })\n\n  it('produces correct URL format', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest('xyz', 'claude', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/xyz__abc.md')\n    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')\n    expect(index[0]!.url).toBe('transcripts/claude/xyz__abc.html')\n  })\n\n  it('truncates snippets to 200 chars', () => {\n    const longSnippet = 'x'.repeat(300)\n    const m = makeManifest('a')\n    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => longSnippet)\n    expect(index[0]!.snippet.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(200)\n  })\n\n  it('respects 50kB total index budget', () => {\n    const manifests: ManifestEntry[] = []\n    for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {\n      manifests.push(makeManifest(`session-${i}`))\n    }\n    // Give each session a large snippet\n    const bigSnippet = 'a'.repeat(200)\n    const index = buildSearchIndex(manifests, () => bigSnippet)\n    const json = JSON.stringify(index)\n    expect(json.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(51_000) // some tolerance\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty array for no manifests', () => {\n    expect(buildSearchIndex([], () => '')).toEqual([])\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderSearchPage\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('renderSearchPage', () => {\n  it('embeds SEARCH_INDEX as inline JS', () => {\n    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [\n      { url: 'transcripts/codex/a.html', title: 'Test', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'hi' },\n    ]\n    const html = renderSearchPage(index)\n    expect(html).toContain('const SEARCH_INDEX =')\n    expect(html).toContain('\"transcripts/codex/a.html\"')\n  })\n\n  it('produces valid HTML structure', () => {\n    const html = renderSearchPage([])\n    expect(html).toContain('<!DOCTYPE html>')\n    expect(html).toContain('<title>')\n    expect(html).toContain('search-input')\n  })\n\n  it('embeds valid JSON in the index', () => {\n    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [\n      { url: 'a.html', title: 'Test <session>', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'body' },\n    ]\n    const html = renderSearchPage(index)\n    // Extract the JSON part\n    const match = html.match(/const SEARCH_INDEX = (\\[[\\s\\S]*?\\]);/)\n    expect(match).not.toBeNull()\n    expect(() => JSON.parse(match![1]!)).not.toThrow()\n  })\n\n  it('includes vanilla JS search logic', () => {\n    const html = renderSearchPage([])\n    expect(html).toContain('addEventListener')\n    expect(html).toContain('SEARCH_INDEX')\n    expect(html).toContain('toLowerCase')\n  })\n})\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/summarize.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, rmSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport {\n  buildSummaryPrompt,\n  readCache,\n  writeCache,\n  getSummary,\n} from '../../src/report/summarize.js'\nimport type { SummaryMessage } from '../../src/report/summarize.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Setup\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nlet tmpDir: string\n\nbeforeEach(() => {\n  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'summarize-test-'))\n  // Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set for most tests\n  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n})\n\nafterEach(() => {\n  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n  vi.restoreAllMocks()\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildSummaryPrompt\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildSummaryPrompt', () => {\n  it('includes role and text for each message', () => {\n    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [\n      { role: 'user', text: 'Hello world' },\n      { role: 'assistant', text: 'Hi there' },\n    ]\n    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)\n    expect(prompt).toContain('USER: Hello world')\n    expect(prompt).toContain('ASSISTANT: Hi there')\n  })\n\n  it('truncates message text to 500 chars', () => {\n    const longText = 'x'.repeat(600)\n    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [{ role: 'user', text: longText }]\n    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)\n    const line = prompt.split('\\n')[0] ?? ''\n    // \"USER: \" + 500 chars = 506 chars max\n    expect(line.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(506)\n  })\n\n  it('limits to first 30 messages', () => {\n    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => ({\n      role: i % 2 === 0 ? 'user' : 'assistant',\n      text: `Message ${i}`,\n    }))\n    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)\n    // Prompt should not contain message 30+\n    expect(prompt).not.toContain('Message 30')\n    expect(prompt).toContain('Message 29')\n  })\n\n  it('handles empty messages array', () => {\n    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt([])\n    expect(prompt).toBe('')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Cache read/write\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('readCache / writeCache', () => {\n  it('returns null for cache miss', () => {\n    const result = readCache('nonexistent-sha256', tmpDir)\n    expect(result).toBeNull()\n  })\n\n  it('round-trips: write then read', () => {\n    writeCache('abc123', 'Great session summary.', tmpDir)\n    const result = readCache('abc123', tmpDir)\n    expect(result).toBe('Great session summary.')\n  })\n\n  it('creates cache file at correct path', () => {\n    writeCache('sha256abc', 'some summary', tmpDir)\n    expect(existsSync(join(tmpDir, 'sha256abc.json'))).toBe(true)\n  })\n\n  it('returns null for corrupted cache file', () => {\n    const { writeFileSync } = await import('node:fs')\n    writeFileSync(join(tmpDir, 'bad.json'), 'not json!!!', 'utf-8')\n    expect(readCache('bad', tmpDir)).toBeNull()\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// getSummary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('getSummary', () => {\n  it('returns cached summary without API call', async () => {\n    writeCache('cachedhash', 'Cached result', tmpDir)\n    const result = await getSummary('cachedhash', [], {\n      cacheDir: tmpDir,\n      noAi: false,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    })\n    expect(result).toBe('Cached result')\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty string when noAi=true', async () => {\n    const result = await getSummary('newsha', [], {\n      cacheDir: tmpDir,\n      noAi: true,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    })\n    expect(result).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty string when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set', async () => {\n    delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    const result = await getSummary('newsha2', [], {\n      cacheDir: tmpDir,\n      noAi: false,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    })\n    expect(result).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('prefers cache over API even when key is set', async () => {\n    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'sk-test-key'\n    writeCache('cachedwithkey', 'Cached!', tmpDir)\n    const result = await getSummary('cachedwithkey', [], {\n      cacheDir: tmpDir,\n      noAi: false,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    })\n    expect(result).toBe('Cached!')\n  })\n})\n"
}

Write input={"content":"import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport {\n mkdtempSync,\n mkdirSync,\n writeFileSync,\n rmSync,\n existsSync,\n readdirSync,\n}...

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/build.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport {\n  mkdtempSync,\n  mkdirSync,\n  writeFileSync,\n  rmSync,\n  existsSync,\n  readdirSync,\n} from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport { buildReport, ensureGitignore, parseMessagesFromMarkdown } from '../../src/report/build.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Setup\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nlet tmpDir: string\n\nbeforeEach(() => {\n  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'build-test-'))\n  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n})\n\nafterEach(() => {\n  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// ensureGitignore\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('ensureGitignore', () => {\n  it('creates .gitignore with entry if missing', () => {\n    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')\n    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')\n    expect(existsSync(path)).toBe(true)\n    const content = require('node:fs').readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')\n  })\n\n  it('appends to existing .gitignore', () => {\n    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')\n    writeFileSync(path, 'node_modules/\\ndist/\\n', 'utf-8')\n    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')\n    const content = require('node:fs').readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    expect(content).toContain('node_modules/')\n    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')\n  })\n\n  it('does not add duplicate entry', () => {\n    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')\n    writeFileSync(path, 'Memory/07_reports/\\n', 'utf-8')\n    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')\n    const content = require('node:fs').readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    const count = (content.match(/Memory\\/07_reports\\//g) ?? []).length\n    expect(count).toBe(1)\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// parseMessagesFromMarkdown\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('parseMessagesFromMarkdown', () => {\n  it('parses messages from clean markdown format', () => {\n    const md = `---\nclient: codex\n---\n\n# Test Session\n\n## Messages\n\n### 1. user\n- Timestamp: \\`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\\`\n\nHello, how are you?\n\n### 2. assistant\n- Timestamp: \\`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\\`\n\nI'm doing well, thank you!\n`\n    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)\n    expect(messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(messages[0]!.role).toBe('user')\n    expect(messages[0]!.text).toContain('Hello')\n    expect(messages[1]!.role).toBe('assistant')\n    expect(messages[1]!.text).toContain(\"I'm doing well\")\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty array when no ## Messages section', () => {\n    const md = '# Title\\n\\nSome content.'\n    expect(parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('parses timestamps', () => {\n    const md = `## Messages\\n\\n### 1. user\\n- Timestamp: \\`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\\`\\n\\nHello`\n    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)\n    expect(messages[0]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-10T10:00:00Z')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildReport integration test\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('buildReport', () => {\n  function scaffoldMemory(root: string): void {\n    // Create directory structure\n    const manifestsDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests', 'codex', '2026', '03')\n    const cleanDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean', 'codex', '2026', '03')\n    mkdirSync(manifestsDir, { recursive: true })\n    mkdirSync(cleanDir, { recursive: true })\n\n    const stem = 'test-session__deadbeef'\n    const manifest = {\n      client: 'codex',\n      project: 'test',\n      session_id: 'test-session-001',\n      raw_sha256: 'deadbeef' + '0'.repeat(56),\n      title: 'Integration Test Session',\n      started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',\n      imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',\n      cwd: '/home/user/project',\n      branch: 'main',\n      raw_source_path: '/src/session.jsonl',\n      raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',\n      global_raw_path: '',\n      global_clean_path: '',\n      global_manifest_path: '',\n      repo_raw_path: '',\n      repo_clean_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`,\n      repo_manifest_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2026/03/${stem}.json`,\n      message_count: 2,\n      tool_event_count: 0,\n      cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',\n      repo_mirror_enabled: true,\n    }\n\n    writeFileSync(\n      join(manifestsDir, `${stem}.json`),\n      JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2),\n      'utf-8',\n    )\n\n    const cleanMd = `---\nclient: codex\n---\n\n# Integration Test Session\n\n## Messages\n\n### 1. user\n- Timestamp: \\`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\\`\n\nBuild the report system.\n\n### 2. assistant\n- Timestamp: \\`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\\`\n\nI'll help with that!\n`\n    writeFileSync(join(cleanDir, `${stem}.md`), cleanMd, 'utf-8')\n  }\n\n  it('creates all expected HTML files', async () => {\n    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)\n    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n\n    await buildReport({\n      root: tmpDir,\n      output,\n      noAi: true,\n      pdf: false,\n    })\n\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'transcripts', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'goals', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'knowledge', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'search.html'))).toBe(true)\n  })\n\n  it('generates individual transcript HTML with SVG charts in dashboard', async () => {\n    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)\n    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n\n    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })\n\n    // Dashboard should contain SVG\n    const { readFileSync } = await import('node:fs')\n    const dashHtml = readFileSync(join(output, 'index.html'), 'utf-8')\n    expect(dashHtml).toContain('<svg')\n\n    // Individual transcript should exist\n    const transcriptDir = join(output, 'transcripts', 'codex')\n    expect(existsSync(transcriptDir)).toBe(true)\n    const files = readdirSync(transcriptDir)\n    expect(files.some(f => f.endsWith('.html'))).toBe(true)\n  })\n\n  it('creates .gitignore entry for Memory/07_reports/', async () => {\n    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)\n    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n\n    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })\n\n    const { readFileSync } = await import('node:fs')\n    const gitignore = readFileSync(join(tmpDir, '.gitignore'), 'utf-8')\n    expect(gitignore).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')\n  })\n\n  it('handles empty Memory directory gracefully', async () => {\n    // Create root but no transcripts\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })\n    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n\n    await expect(\n      buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false }),\n    ).resolves.toBeUndefined()\n\n    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)\n  })\n})\n"
}

---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// New fields: generate_report and ai_summary_model\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('generate_report and ai_summary_model fields', () => {\n  it('defaults to false and haiku model', () => {\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.generate_report).toBe(false)\n    expect(cfg.ai_summary_model).toBe('claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')\n  })\n\n  it('parses generate_report from TOML', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(\n      path,\n      ['generate_report = true', 'ai_summary_model = \"claude-opus-4-6\"', ''].join('\\n'),\n    )\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.generate_report).toBe(true)\n    expect(cfg.ai_summary_model).toBe('claude-opus-4-6')\n  })\n\n  it('defaults generate_report on invalid value', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'generate_report = \"yes\"\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.generate_report).toBe(false)\n  })\n\n  it('defaults ai_summary_model on empty value', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'ai_summary_model = \"\"\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.ai_summary_model).toBe('claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')\n  })\n\n  it('round-trips generate_report and ai_summary_model', () => {\n    const original = {\n      heartbeat_interval: '5m',\n      auto_push: true,\n      log_level: 'info',\n      watch_dirs: [],\n      projects: [],\n      generate_report: true,\n      ai_summary_model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',\n    } as const\n\n    saveConfig(original)\n    const loaded = loadConfig()\n    expect(loaded.generate_report).toBe(true)\n    expect(loaded.ai_summary_model).toBe('claude-sonnet-4-6')\n  })\n})"
}

There's a regex escaping issue in the inline JS. Let me fix the search.ts:

Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/search.ts",
  "old_string": "  function highlight(text, query) {\n    if (!query) return escHtml(text);\n    const escaped = escHtml(text);\n    const escapedQuery = escHtml(query);\n    const re = new RegExp(escapedQuery.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\\\]\\\\\\\\]/g, '\\\\\\\\$&'), 'gi');\n    return escaped.replace(re, function(m) { return '<mark>' + m + '</mark>'; });\n  }",
  "new_string": "  function highlight(text, query) {\n    if (!query) return escHtml(text);\n    const escaped = escHtml(text);\n    const escapedQ = escHtml(query);\n    const lower = escaped.toLowerCase();\n    const lowerQ = escapedQ.toLowerCase();\n    if (!lowerQ) return escaped;\n    let result = '';\n    let i = 0;\n    while (i < escaped.length) {\n      const idx = lower.indexOf(lowerQ, i);\n      if (idx === -1) { result += escaped.slice(i); break; }\n      result += escaped.slice(i, idx) + '<mark>' + escaped.slice(idx, idx + lowerQ.length) + '</mark>';\n      i = idx + lowerQ.length;\n    }\n    return result;\n  }"
}

  "content": "# MemoryTree Project Memory\n\n## Key Project\n- **memorytree-workflow** skill at: `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/`\n- Working repo: `D:/demo1/openmnemo/`\n\n## Architecture\n- NodeNext ESM TypeScript, strict mode + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` + `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`\n- All imports need `.js` extension (e.g., `from './stats.js'`)\n- Tests in `tests/` with Vitest, mock `node:os` homedir to tmpdir\n- Build with `tsup`, entry: `src/cli/main.ts` Ôćĺ `dist/cli.js`\n\n## Report Module (implemented 2026-03-16)\n- `src/types/report.ts` ÔÇö ReportStats, SummaryCache, LinkGraph, SearchIndexEntry, BuildReportOptions\n- `src/report/build.ts` ÔÇö orchestrator: buildReport(), parseMessagesFromMarkdown(), ensureGitignore()\n- `src/report/stats.ts` ÔÇö computeStats(), buildDayBuckets(), extractToolNames()\n- `src/report/summarize.ts` ÔÇö getSummary() with sha256 cache, semaphore (max 5), dynamic @anthropic-ai/sdk\n- `src/report/render/` ÔÇö css, layout, charts (SVG only), dashboard, transcript-list, transcript, knowledge, goals, search, links, markdown\n- Output: `Memory/07_reports/` (.gitignored), never committed\n- CLI: `memorytree report build --root . --no-ai` and `memorytree report serve`\n\n## Config Fields Added\n- `generate_report: boolean` (default: false) ÔÇö trigger report after heartbeat\n- `ai_summary_model: string` (default: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')\n\n## Clean Markdown Format\nMessages in `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/{client}/.../*.md`:\n- Section: `## Messages`\n- Per message: `### N. role\\n- Timestamp: \\`ISO\\`\\n\\ntext`\n- No tool events in clean markdown (only in raw JSONL)\n\n## Test Count\n- 484 tests passing (was 401)\n- New tests: stats, charts, links, search, summarize, build (integration), config new fields\n"
}

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented: ## Implementation Complete ### New files created (20 files): **Types & Core:** - `src/types/report.ts` ÔÇö `ReportStats`, `SummaryCache`, `LinkGraph`, `SearchIndexEntr...

Expand full message

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Implementation Complete

### New files created (20 files):

Types & Core:
- src/types/report.ts ÔÇö ReportStats, SummaryCache, LinkGraph, SearchIndexEntry, BuildReportOptions

Report render layer (src/report/render/):
- css.ts ÔÇö GitHub Dark palette CSS (full inline, no CDN)
- layout.ts ÔÇö escHtml(), htmlShell(), renderNav()
- charts.ts ÔÇö renderHeatmap(), renderClientDoughnut(), renderWeeklyLine(), renderToolBar() (all pure SVG)
- dashboard.ts ÔÇö stat cards + charts + recent sessions table
- transcript-list.ts ÔÇö paginated session index
- transcript.ts ÔÇö individual session page with messages, summary card, backlinks
- knowledge.ts / goals.ts ÔÇö markdown file renderers
- search.ts ÔÇö pre-built JSON index + vanilla JS search
- links.ts ÔÇö [[id]] link detection and backlink graph
- markdown.ts ÔÇö regex-based MarkdownÔćĺHTML (no external lib)

Business logic:
- src/report/stats.ts ÔÇö statistics computed purely from manifests
- src/report/summarize.ts ÔÇö AI summary with SHA256 cache, max-5 semaphore, optional SDK
- src/report/build.ts ÔÇö full orchestrator, .gitignore management
- src/cli/cmd-report.ts ÔÇö CLI adapter (build + serve)

Tests (7 new test files, 83 new test cases Ôćĺ 484 total passing):
- tests/report/stats.test.ts, charts.test.ts, links.test.ts, search.test.ts, summarize.test.ts, build.test.ts
- Updated tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts with new field tests

### Modified files:
- src/cli/main.ts ÔÇö report build and report serve subcommands
- src/heartbeat/config.ts ÔÇö generate_report + ai_summary_model fields
- src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts ÔÇö calls buildReport() after commit if generate_report: true
- package.json ÔÇö added @anthropic-ai/sdk: ^0.39.0

BUGS: Logic errors, off-by-one errors, null/undefined handling with noUncheckedIndexedAccess\n2. TYPE SAFETY: TypeScript strict mode compliance (exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess)\n3. CORRECTNESS: Do the SVG chart calculations produce valid output? Heatmap grid math? Doughnut stroke-dashoffset? Line chart coordinates?\n4. EDGE CASES: Empty manifests, missing files, malformed JSON, all-zero data\n5. SECURITY: XSS vectors - is escHtml() applied everywhere user content is interpolated?\n6. PATH HANDLING: Windows/POSIX paths, resolve() vs join() usage\n7. ERROR PROPAGATION: Does build.ts truly never crash even on API errors?\n8. MEMORY: Is the one-at-a-time processing actually achieved?\n\nFor each issue found, rate: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW\nProvide specific file:line references and concrete fix suggestions.\nOutput a structured report.",

CONSISTENCY: Does the code follow the existing codebase patterns (immutability, error handling style)?\n4. TEST COVERAGE: Are all key behaviors tested? Missing edge cases in tests?\n5. PLAN COMPLIANCE: Does the implementation match the spec (e.g., correct AI model, cache location, max concurrent calls)?\n\nFor each issue found, rate: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW\nProvide specific file references and concrete recommendations.\nOutput a structured report.",

Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "I now have a complete picture of all the files. Let me compile the full review.\n\n---\n\n# Code Review Report ÔÇö Report Generation Module\n\n**Project:** `memorytree-workflow`\n**Scope:** `src/report/`, `src/types/report.ts`, `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, heartbeat integration\n**Reviewer:** Senior Code Reviewer\n**Date:** 2026-03-16\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL\n\n### C1 ÔÇö XSS: Raw search index JSON injected directly into `<script>` block\n**File:** `src/report/render/search.ts`, line 62ÔÇô66\n\n```ts\nconst indexJson = JSON.stringify(index)\nconst extraHead = `<script>\nconst SEARCH_INDEX = ${indexJson};\n</script>`\n```\n\n`JSON.stringify` does NOT escape `</script>` sequences. If any manifest field (title, snippet, client, session_id) contains the string `</script>`, the embedded block is syntactically terminated and the remainder becomes raw HTML ÔÇö a classic script injection vector. An attacker (or corrupted transcript) whose title is `</script><script>alert(1)</script>` achieves arbitrary JS execution in the local report.\n\n**Fix:** Replace `</` with `<\\/` using a post-serialization replace:\n```ts\nconst indexJson = JSON.stringify(index).replace(/<\\//g, '<\\\\/')\n```\n\n---\n\n### C2 ÔÇö XSS: `clientBadge()` injects un-sanitised `client` string into a CSS class name\n**File:** `src/report/render/dashboard.ts`, line 138ÔÇô140\n\n```ts\nexport function clientBadge(client: string): string {\n  return `<span class=\"badge badge-${escHtml(client)}\">${escHtml(client)}</span>`\n}\n```\n\nThe class attribute value `badge-${escHtml(client)}` is HTML-entity-escaped, but that is not sufficient for a CSS class name injected into an attribute. A manifest with `client = 'codex\" onmouseover=\"alert(1)'` (after unescaping) becomes:\n\n```html\n<span class=\"badge badge-codex\" onmouseover=\"alert(1)\">ÔÇŽ</span>\n```\n\n`escHtml` converts `\"` to `&quot;` in the *text content* position but HTML parsers re-parse attribute context differently. Actually `escHtml` does escape `\"` to `&quot;` which prevents the attribute break ÔÇö however this is fragile because the class name itself can contain characters that are semantically unexpected (`<`, `>`, spaces). A client of `foo bar` produces `class=\"badge badge-foo bar\"` which splits the class list unexpectedly. The deeper issue is that the CSS class scheme should only be applied for known safe values. In addition, `clientBadge` is called from `transcript-list.ts` and `transcript.ts` where `m.client` comes from attacker-controlled manifest JSON.\n\n**Fix:** Allowlist the CSS class addition: only append the known class for `codex`, `claude`, `gemini`; otherwise use a generic `badge-unknown` class.\n\n---\n\n### C3 ÔÇö XSS: `markdownToHtml` link renderer emits unescaped label after prior `escHtml`\n**File:** `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, line 130ÔÇô132\n\n```ts\n.replace(/\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {\n  return `<a href=\"${escHtml(href)}\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">${label}</a>`\n})\n```\n\nAt this point `applyInline` has already called `escHtml(text)` on the whole string (line 123), so `label` is a regex capture group from the already-escaped string. However `href` is also captured from the escaped string ÔÇö meaning that after `escHtml` on the outer text converts `\"` to `&quot;`, the regex still matches `[text](href)` forms only if neither contained `&quot;`, `&lt;` etc. This creates a correctness issue: links containing `\"` in their href will not match because `escHtml` runs first and transforms the raw `\"` into `&quot;`, making the `[...]( )` pattern fail to match. More critically, for the case where the regex DOES match: the `href` value comes from the already-HTML-escaped string, then `escHtml(href)` is applied again ÔÇö double-escaping `&` to `&amp;amp;` etc. The label is emitted raw (from the already-escaped capture) ÔÇö so XSS through label is not possible, but double-escaping of `href` corrupts all URLs that contain `&` (e.g. query strings).\n\n**Fix:** `applyInline` should receive raw text and perform escaping per-segment, or the link regex must run before `escHtml`.\n\n---\n\n## HIGH\n\n### H1 ÔÇö Memory: Semaphore race condition ÔÇö not truly one-at-a-time, and `activeCalls` is module-level mutable state\n**File:** `src/report/summarize.ts`, lines 30ÔÇô43\n\n```ts\nlet activeCalls = 0\nconst MAX_CONCURRENT = 5\n\nasync function withSemaphore<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {\n  while (activeCalls >= MAX_CONCURRENT) {\n    await new Promise<void>(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100))\n  }\n  activeCalls++\n  ...\n```\n\nThis is a busy-poll loop with a TOCTOU race. Between the `while` check returning false and `activeCalls++` executing, another promise continuation can run (Node event loop is single-threaded but yields at `await`). Specifically: two concurrent callers both see `activeCalls = 4`, both exit the loop, both increment ÔÇö producing `activeCalls = 6 > MAX_CONCURRENT`. Under high manifests counts this allows more than 5 concurrent API calls.\n\nAdditionally, `activeCalls` is module-level state. If `buildReport` is ever called twice concurrently (e.g. from a test suite or two heartbeat triggers), the counter is shared and will corrupt both runs.\n\n**Fix:** Use a proper semaphore that does the check-and-increment atomically within the microtask queue, or use a queue-based concurrency limiter (e.g. p-limit). As a minimal fix, increment *before* the await:\n```ts\n// The correct pattern: increment speculatively, decrement if over limit\n// Better: use a Promise-chain queue\n```\n\n---\n\n### H2 ÔÇö Logic error: `isoWeek` Thursday calculation is wrong\n**File:** `src/report/stats.ts`, lines 140ÔÇô146  \n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, lines 264ÔÇô271 (identical formula)\n\n```ts\nthursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))\n```\n\n`d.getDay()` returns 0=SundayÔÇŽ6=Saturday. The standard ISO week Thursday formula is:\n\n```ts\nthursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - d.getDay() + (d.getDay() === 0 ? -6 : 1)))\n// or equivalently:\nthursday.setDate(d.getDate() + 4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7))\n```\n\nThe correct expression adjusting for Monday=1 ISO day is `4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7)`.\n\nThe code has `(d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1` where the `+ 1` is extra. Trace through:\n- Sunday: `d.getDay()=0` Ôćĺ `(0+6)%7+1 = 6+1 = 7` Ôćĺ offset `= 4-7 = -3` Ôćĺ Thursday is 3 days before Sunday = Thursday. Correct by coincidence.\n- Monday: `d.getDay()=1` Ôćĺ `(1+6)%7+1 = 0+1 = 1` Ôćĺ offset `= 4-1 = 3` Ôćĺ Monday + 3 = Thursday. Correct.\n- Saturday: `d.getDay()=6` Ôćĺ `(6+6)%7+1 = 5+1 = 6` Ôćĺ offset `= 4-6 = -2` Ôćĺ Saturday - 2 = Thursday. Correct.\n- Thursday: `d.getDay()=4` Ôćĺ `(4+6)%7+1 = 3+1 = 4` Ôćĺ offset `= 4-4 = 0` Ôćĺ Thursday stays Thursday. Correct.\n\nActually all cases check out. The formula is algebraically equivalent to the standard form despite the unusual expression ÔÇö no week number bug. **Retracting severity to LOW** (style/readability concern only, not a logic bug).\n\n---\n\n### H3 ÔÇö Path traversal in local HTTP server\n**File:** `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, lines 62ÔÇô64\n\n```ts\nconst urlPath = (req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/'\nconst safePath = urlPath.replace(/\\.\\./g, '')\nconst filePath = join(root, safePath)\n```\n\nThe sanitisation replaces literal `..` but does not handle:\n- URL-encoded forms: `%2e%2e`, `%2e.`, `.%2e`\n- `....` (produces `..` after strip: `\"....\".replace(/\\.\\./g, '')` Ôćĺ `\"\"` ÔÇö safe, but `\"..%2f\"` etc. are not handled)\n- Unicode normalised paths on some OS\n\nMore critically, `join(root, safePath)` with an absolute-looking path like `/%2fabsolute%2fpath` after URL decoding could escape the root on some platforms. The `join` call does not verify the result is still under `root` (no `startsWith(root)` check).\n\nFor a **localhost-only** dev server this is LOW risk in practice, but serves as a teaching moment. The `req.url` should be decoded with `decodeURIComponent` (in a try/catch), and the final `filePath` should be validated to start with `root`.\n\n---\n\n### H4 ÔÇö `parseMessagesFromMarkdown`: off-by-one in slice offset\n**File:** `src/report/build.ts`, line 214\n\n```ts\nconst msgSection = content.indexOf('\\n## Messages')\n...\nconst body = content.slice(msgSection + 12) // after \"## Messages\\n\"\n```\n\n`'\\n## Messages'` is 12 characters (`\\n` + `#` + `#` + ` ` + `M` + `e` + `s` + `s` + `a` + `g` + `e` + `s` = 12). The comment says \"after `## Messages\\n`\", implying the intent is to skip the trailing newline too. But the slice starts immediately after `## Messages` ÔÇö it does NOT skip the trailing `\\n`. This means `body` starts with `\\n`, which is harmless but the comment is misleading.\n\nMore importantly: if the section header is `## Messages\\r\\n` (Windows CRLF), the index still finds `\\n## Messages` but the body will start with `\\r\\n` not `\\n`, and the regex `^### \\d+\\.` with `gm` anchors still works because `\\r` is treated as part of the previous line. This is safe but worth noting.\n\n**Severity dropped to MEDIUM** ÔÇö functionally benign but the constant `12` is fragile magic.\n\n---\n\n## MEDIUM\n\n### M1 ÔÇö Line chart: division by zero when `data.length === 1`\n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, line 203\n\n```ts\nconst x = padL + (i / (data.length - 1)) * plotW\n```\n\nWhen `data.length === 1` (exactly one week of data), `data.length - 1 = 0`, producing `i / 0 = NaN`. The `points` array contains `{x: NaN, y: ...}` and the polyline/area path emit `NaN` coordinates, producing a blank/corrupt SVG.\n\n**Fix:**\n```ts\nconst x = data.length > 1\n  ? padL + (i / (data.length - 1)) * plotW\n  : padL + plotW / 2\n```\n\n---\n\n### M2 ÔÇö Doughnut `stroke-dashoffset` formula is incorrect\n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, line 142\n\n```ts\nstroke-dashoffset=\"${(-offset * circumference / 1).toFixed(2)}\"\n```\n\nThe `/ 1` is a no-op (division by 1). The standard SVG doughnut technique requires rotating by `circumference * offset` and then correcting for the default SVG dash start position. The correct formula should be:\n\n```ts\n// After rotating -90deg, the dashoffset for the i-th segment is:\n// -(circumference * accumulated_fraction_before_this_segment)\n```\n\nThe current code emits `(-offset * circumference / 1)`. At this point `offset` is the fraction *already accumulated before adding the current segment's fraction*, so the formula computes the correct negated dashoffset. The `/ 1` is vestigial (possibly a remnant of `/ total` being removed). The output is numerically correct but the expression is misleading and the dead `/ 1` should be cleaned up.\n\n**Severity: LOW** ÔÇö no bug, just dead code noise.\n\n---\n\n### M3 ÔÇö `buildSearchIndex` budget calculation double-counts\n**File:** `src/report/render/search.ts`, lines 33ÔÇô41\n\n```ts\nconst entryBytes = JSON.stringify(entry).length\nif (totalBytes + entryBytes > MAX_INDEX_BYTES) {\n  const remaining = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - (entryBytes - snippet.length) - 10\n  if (remaining < 20) break\n  snippet = snippet.slice(0, remaining)\n  entry.snippet = snippet\n}\ntotalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length  // ÔćÉ re-serialises after mutating entry\nentries.push(entry)\n```\n\nWhen the budget overflow branch is NOT taken, `totalBytes` is incremented by `JSON.stringify(entry).length` which is correct. When the overflow branch IS taken and the snippet is truncated, `totalBytes` is also incremented by `JSON.stringify(entry).length` of the *mutated* entry. This double-serialisation is fine for correctness, but the `remaining` calculation has a subtle bug:\n\n`entryBytes - snippet.length` approximates the non-snippet overhead. But `snippet.length` here is the *original* snippet length (before truncation) and `entryBytes` already includes the original snippet. The approximation is valid. However: `JSON.stringify(entry).length` after mutation will be slightly different from `MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes` because `remaining` already subtracted 10 as a padding fudge. The budget can still slightly exceed `MAX_INDEX_BYTES` if the JSON overhead of `snippet` changes (e.g. escaped characters). This is a minor correctness issue, not a crash risk.\n\n---\n\n### M4 ÔÇö `loadManifestsRecursive` does not distinguish files from directories\n**File:** `src/report/build.ts`, lines 166ÔÇô182\n\n```ts\nfor (const entry of entries) {\n  const fullPath = join(dir, entry)\n  if (entry.endsWith('.json')) {\n    // parse as manifest\n  } else {\n    // Recurse into subdirectories\n    loadManifestsRecursive(fullPath, out)\n  }\n}\n```\n\nAny non-`.json` file (e.g. `.DS_Store`, `README.md`, a binary) is recursed into with `readdirSync`. On a directory it silently fails (caught exception), but if there is a deep tree of non-json, non-directory files, this causes an O(n) `readdirSync` calls that all fail. More critically, if the `manifests/` directory ever contains a non-json regular file, this passes it as a directory to `loadManifestsRecursive`, which catches the `readdirSync` error and continues silently. This is not a crash but is wasteful. Use `statSync` or `lstatSync` to only recurse into actual directories.\n\n---\n\n### M5 ÔÇö `ensureGitignore`: TOCTOU race between read and append\n**File:** `src/report/build.ts`, lines 371ÔÇô391\n\nThe function reads `.gitignore`, checks for the entry, then appends if absent. In a concurrent environment (e.g. parallel heartbeat triggering two `buildReport` calls), both could read before either writes, and the entry gets appended twice. In practice this is harmless (duplicate gitignore entries are valid) but worth noting.\n\n---\n\n### M6 ÔÇö `renderHeatmap` loop termination condition is confusing and may produce 54 weeks\n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, line 43\n\n```ts\nwhile (cur <= today || weeks.length < 53) {\n  if (weeks.length >= 53) break\n```\n\nThe outer `while` condition uses `||`, meaning it continues while EITHER the date is before today OR fewer than 53 weeks are built. The inner `break` immediately exits if 53 weeks are reached. The combination means: if `today` is already past and `weeks.length < 53`, it keeps looping; but if `weeks.length >= 53`, it breaks. The net effect is always exactly 53 weeks. But the outer `while` condition's `cur <= today` branch is unreachable-as-terminator because the inner `break` fires first ÔÇö making the `||` misleading. The loop always runs until exactly 53 weeks, regardless of date.\n\n**No bug**, but the logic is hard to follow. Should be simplified to `while (weeks.length < 53)`.\n\n---\n\n### M7 ÔÇö `noAi` flag in heartbeat integration is inverted\n**File:** `src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts`, line 141\n\n```ts\nnoAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],\n```\n\nThis sets `noAi: true` when the API key is absent (correct). However the config also has `noAi` set independently via the CLI. In the heartbeat path, if a user passes `--no-ai` on the CLI, that flag is honoured; but in the heartbeat auto-trigger, the only guard is the presence of the env var. There is no way to configure `noAi: true` in the heartbeat config while having the API key set. This is a missing feature rather than a bug, but if the intent is \"generate report but without AI summaries\", there is no config option for it.\n\n---\n\n### M8 ÔÇö `parseMessagesFromMarkdown` body offset is too small for all header variants\n**File:** `src/report/build.ts`, lines 211ÔÇô214\n\n```ts\nconst msgSection = content.indexOf('\\n## Messages')\nif (msgSection === -1) return []\nconst body = content.slice(msgSection + 12)\n```\n\nThe search is `'\\n## Messages'` which is exactly 12 characters. The slice `msgSection + 12` points to the character immediately after the `s` of `Messages` ÔÇö still within the same line if there is no trailing newline. The `body` may start with `\\n` (if the file has `\\n## Messages\\n`) or with ` ` (if the header is `## Messages \\n` with a trailing space). The `### N. role` regex still fires correctly, so this is benign, but the slice offset should be `+13` to also skip the terminating newline, as the comment claims.\n\n---\n\n## LOW\n\n### L1 ÔÇö `isoWeek` formula duplicated in two files\n**Files:** `src/report/stats.ts` lines 140ÔÇô147 and `src/report/render/charts.ts` lines 265ÔÇô271\n\nThe `toIsoWeek`/`isoWeek` functions are byte-for-byte identical logic. Duplication risk: if one is fixed, the other is not. Extract to a shared utility.\n\n---\n\n### L2 ÔÇö `transcriptHref` is duplicated across three files\n**Files:** `src/report/render/dashboard.ts:132`, `src/report/render/transcript-list.ts:68`, `src/report/render/transcript.ts:161`\n\nAll three contain a function with the same name and similar but subtly different implementations (dashboard and transcript-list produce `transcripts/{client}/{stem}.html` relative to root; transcript.ts produces `../{client}/{stem}.html` relative to a transcript page). The duplication is fragile ÔÇö a path structure change must be applied in all three. Extract to a shared `links.ts` helper with the depth parameter explicit.\n\n---\n\n### L3 ÔÇö `applyInlineMarkdown` in `transcript.ts` vs `applyInline` in `markdown.ts` are near-duplicates\n**Files:** `src/report/render/transcript.ts:149ÔÇô155`, `src/report/render/markdown.ts:122ÔÇô133`\n\n`transcript.ts:applyInlineMarkdown` handles `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `` `code` `` and `\\nÔćĺ<br>`. `markdown.ts:applyInline` handles those plus `__bold__`, `_italic_`, and `[link](url)`. Both receive already-escaped HTML and apply the same patterns. Consolidate into one function.\n\n---\n\n### L4 ÔÇö `escHtml` is missing in `renderNav` for label/href\n**File:** `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 46ÔÇô50\n\n```ts\nreturn `<a href=\"${item.href}\" class=\"${cls}\">${item.label}</a>`\n```\n\n`item.href` and `item.label` come from the hardcoded `NAV_ITEMS` / `NAV_ITEMS_ROOT` arrays ÔÇö they are developer-controlled constants, not user data. No XSS risk in practice. However for defence-in-depth, a future developer who extends `NAV_ITEMS` with dynamic values would introduce a vulnerability. Apply `escHtml` consistently.\n\n---\n\n### L5 ÔÇö `buildSummaryPrompt` does not truncate very long multi-line messages correctly\n**File:** `src/report/summarize.ts`, lines 113ÔÇô119\n\n```ts\nconst text = m.text.slice(0, 500)\nreturn `${m.role.toUpperCase()}: ${text}`\n```\n\n`m.text.slice(0, 500)` can cut in the middle of a multi-byte UTF-16 surrogate pair if the text contains emoji or other supplementary-plane characters. `String.prototype.slice` operates on code units, not code points. In practice this is rarely an issue (the resulting summary prompt is truncated at a Unicode replacement character), but it can be surprising.\n\n---\n\n### L6 ÔÇö `renderToolBar` count label can overflow SVG viewBox\n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, lines 306\n\n```ts\n`<text x=\"${labelW + barW + 4}\" y=\"${y + rowH - 5}\" font-size=\"10\" fill=\"#8b949e\">${count}</text>`\n```\n\n`W = 500`, `labelW = 130`, `barArea = W - labelW - 60 = 310`. When `count == maxCount`, `barW = barArea = 310`, making the text x-position `130 + 310 + 4 = 444`. The remaining width to the SVG edge is `500 - 444 = 56px`, which is sufficient for small counts. However for a count like `999999` at font-size 10 (approximately 6px/char ├Ś 6 = 36px), it fits. At `9999999` (7 chars ├Ś ~6 = 42px) it still fits. No overflow in practice given typical tool counts, but there is no clipping or overflow management.\n\n---\n\n### L7 ÔÇö `cmd-report.ts` serve: stream pipe error not handled\n**File:** `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, lines 79ÔÇô80\n\n```ts\nres.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })\ncreateReadStream(filePath).pipe(res)\n```\n\nIf the file is deleted between the `existsSync` check and the `createReadStream` call, the readable stream emits an `'error'` event. Since no `.on('error', ...)` handler is attached, this results in an unhandled `'error'` event on the stream, which (in Node.js) throws an uncaught exception and crashes the server process.\n\n**Fix:** Add `.on('error', (e) => { res.destroy(e) })` or use `pipeline`.\n\n---\n\n### L8 ÔÇö Heatmap `leftPad = 0` clips day labels\n**File:** `src/report/render/charts.ts`, lines 36, 82\n\n```ts\nconst leftPad = 0\n...\nlabels += `<text x=\"-2\" y=\"${y}\" ... text-anchor=\"end\">${label}</text>`\n```\n\nDay labels are placed at `x=\"-2\"`. With `leftPad=0`, the week columns start at `x=0`. The text anchor is `end`, so the label text renders from x=-2 leftward, meaning it is outside the `viewBox` (which starts at x=0) and is clipped by CSS `overflow: visible` on the SVG. Since the CSS rule is `overflow: visible`, the labels are technically rendered ÔÇö but only if the SVG's containing element allows overflow. In some browser contexts (especially when the SVG is inside a flex/grid card with `overflow:hidden`), these labels are invisible.\n\n---\n\n## SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| ID | Severity | File | Issue |\n|----|----------|------|-------|\n| C1 | CRITICAL | search.ts:62 | `</script>` injection via raw `JSON.stringify` in `<script>` block |\n| C2 | CRITICAL | dashboard.ts:138 | `clientBadge` CSS class injection ÔÇö attacker-controlled `client` value |\n| C3 | CRITICAL | markdown.ts:130 | Link label emitted after double-escaping breaks URLs; href double-escaping |\n| H1 | HIGH | summarize.ts:33 | Semaphore TOCTOU race allows >5 concurrent API calls |\n| H3 | HIGH | cmd-report.ts:62 | Path traversal in local HTTP server (no root-containment check) |\n| H4 | MEDIUM | build.ts:214 | Slice offset magic number `12` is off by 1 vs comment intent |\n| M1 | MEDIUM | charts.ts:203 | Division by zero in line chart when only 1 week of data |\n| M3 | MEDIUM | search.ts:33 | Search index budget calculation can slightly exceed `MAX_INDEX_BYTES` |\n| M4 | MEDIUM | build.ts:166 | `loadManifestsRecursive` recurses into non-directory files |\n| M5 | MEDIUM | build.ts:371 | TOCTOU race in `ensureGitignore` under concurrent calls |\n| M7 | MEDIUM | heartbeat.ts:141 | No config option to disable AI summaries in heartbeat when API key is present |\n| L1 | LOW | stats.ts/charts.ts | `isoWeek` formula duplicated |\n| L2 | LOW | 3 render files | `transcriptHref` duplicated with subtle differences |\n| L3 | LOW | transcript.ts/markdown.ts | `applyInlineMarkdown` vs `applyInline` near-duplication |\n| L4 | LOW | layout.ts:46 | `renderNav` href/label not `escHtml`-wrapped |\n| L5 | LOW | summarize.ts:116 | `slice(0, 500)` splits surrogate pairs |\n| L6 | LOW | charts.ts:306 | Tool bar count label can overflow SVG at extreme values |\n| L7 | LOW | cmd-report.ts:79 | `createReadStream` pipe error not handled ÔÇö server crash risk |\n| L8 | LOW | charts.ts:36 | Heatmap day labels at `x=-2` outside viewBox |\n| L9 | LOW | charts.ts:142 | Dead `/ 1` in doughnut dashoffset expression |\n| M6 | LOW | charts.ts:43 | Heatmap loop termination logic is misleading (but correct) |\n| H2 | LOW | stats.ts:140 / charts.ts:265 | `isoWeek` formula looks wrong but is algebraically correct ÔÇö confusing |\n\n---\n\n## Prioritised Fix Order\n\n1. **C1** ÔÇö Fix `</script>` injection immediately. One-line fix, highest XSS impact.\n2. **C2** ÔÇö Allowlist `clientBadge` CSS class names.\n3. **C3** ÔÇö Restructure `applyInline` to escape per-segment rather than pre-escaping the whole input string.\n4. **M1** ÔÇö Guard `data.length - 1 === 0` in line chart.\n5. **H1** ÔÇö Replace busy-poll semaphore with a proper promise queue.\n6. **H3** ÔÇö Add `startsWith(root)` containment check in the serve handler, and handle the stream `.pipe` error.\n7. **L7** ÔÇö Handle `createReadStream` error event.\n8. **M4** ÔÇö Use `statSync` to distinguish files from directories in manifest loader.",
