  {
    "text": "I now have a complete picture of the entire module. Let me compile the full review.\n\n---\n\n# Architecture & Code Quality Review ÔÇö Report Generation Module\n\n**Project:** `memorytree-workflow` at `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/`\n**Reviewer scope:** 21 source + test files, ~1,600 lines of production code, ~700 lines of tests\n\n---\n\n## 1. ARCHITECTURE\n\n### 1.1 Module coupling and single responsibility\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö `build.ts` is doing too much**\n\n`src/report/build.ts` (393 lines) is the orchestrator, but it also owns three responsibilities that should be in separate modules:\n\n- Markdown message parsing (`parseMessagesFromMarkdown`, `parseCleanMarkdownMessages`) ÔÇö currently exported from the orchestrator, used by tests and by `transcript.ts` indirectly\n- Raw JSONL tool-extraction (`extractToolSummariesFromRaw`) ÔÇö a non-trivial parser living inside the build coordinator\n- `.gitignore` mutation (`ensureGitignore`) ÔÇö a repo-management utility that belongs either in a `utils/git.ts` file or the existing CLI layer\n\nThe result is that `build.ts` imports from nearly every other module in the subsystem (8 distinct imports) while also defining core parsing logic. A future developer wanting to understand \"how transcripts are parsed\" must look inside the orchestrator, not a dedicated parser module.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `dashboard.ts` exports `clientBadge` for use by sibling renderers**\n\n`transcript-list.ts` and `transcript.ts` both import `clientBadge` from `dashboard.ts`. This creates a coupling between sibling render modules through what should be a shared utility. `clientBadge` belongs in `layout.ts` or a small `render/utils.ts` alongside `escHtml` and `renderNav`, not in a page-level renderer.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö Nav path encoding is fragile**\n\n`layout.ts` has two hardcoded nav arrays (`NAV_ITEMS` and `NAV_ITEMS_ROOT`) differentiated by a `rootLevel: boolean` flag. The `../` prefix on all non-root hrefs assumes a single level of nesting. Transcripts are at `transcripts/{client}/{stem}.html` ÔÇö two levels deep ÔÇö but `renderNav` is called with `rootLevel = false` which produces one-level-up hrefs. This actually works only because the nav bar links to index pages one level up, but the assumption is invisible and breaks if the nesting depth ever increases or if a page is served from a deeper path.\n\n---\n\n## 2. CODE QUALITY\n\n### 2.1 File sizes\n\nAll files are well within limits. The largest production file is `build.ts` at 393 lines, `charts.ts` at 315 lines, and `css.ts` at 346 lines (primarily a string literal). All others are under 175 lines. No file approaches the 800-line ceiling.\n\n### 2.2 Function lengths\n\nAll functions are under 50 lines with two exceptions:\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `renderHeatmap` in `charts.ts` is 68 lines (lines 24ÔÇô92)**\n\nStill readable, but could be split: date-range generation and cell-rect generation are independent concerns.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `parseMessagesFromMarkdown` in `build.ts` is 55 lines (lines 207ÔÇô261)**\n\nJust over the 50-line guideline. Not a meaningful problem, but worth noting.\n\n### 2.3 Magic numbers and hardcoded values\n\n**LOW ÔÇö Hardcoded pixel constants in `charts.ts`**\n\n`renderHeatmap` and `renderClientDoughnut` contain magic layout numbers (`cellSize = 11`, `cx = 90`, `r = 60`, `innerR = 38`, etc.) with no named constants. These are grouped coherently but are not self-documenting.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö Hardcoded color strings duplicated between `css.ts` and `charts.ts`**\n\n`HEATMAP_COLORS` in `charts.ts` duplicates the `--green-*` CSS variable values. If the theme colors change, both files must be updated. SVG cannot use CSS variables directly, so this is a known trade-off, but it should at minimum be documented.\n\n---\n\n## 3. CONSISTENCY\n\n### 3.1 Immutability\n\nThe codebase follows the immutability guideline well overall.\n\n**HIGH ÔÇö `links.ts` mutates arrays in-place (lines 50ÔÇô64)**\n\n```ts\nconst fwd = forwardLinks[m.session_id]\nif (fwd) {\n  if (!fwd.includes(targetId)) fwd.push(targetId)   // mutation\n} else {\n  forwardLinks[m.session_id] = [targetId]\n}\n```\n\nThe arrays stored in `forwardLinks` and `backlinks` are mutated with `.push()`. The project coding standard is \"NEVER mutate existing ones ÔÇö return new copies.\" The immutable pattern here would be either `forwardLinks[id] = [...existing, targetId]` or using a `Map<string, Set<string>>` to deduplicate. This is the most obvious consistency violation in the entire module.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö Module-level mutable state in `summarize.ts` (line 30)**\n\n```ts\nlet activeCalls = 0\n```\n\nThis is a module-level integer incremented and decremented by concurrent promises. It works correctly as written (single Node.js process, event loop is not preemptive), but it is a hidden side effect. A semaphore implemented as a class or a pure-functional token bucket would be safer and more testable. The current pattern is also not reset between tests ÔÇö if the test runner ever runs `getSummary` in a mode where the API is reachable, `activeCalls` could leak across tests.\n\n### 3.2 Error handling\n\nError handling is consistent and conservative throughout: all I/O is wrapped in try/catch and failures are silently skipped to avoid aborting the build. This matches the stated goal of resilience. The only concern is:\n\n**LOW ÔÇö Suppressed errors lose diagnostic information in `build.ts` (lines 76ÔÇô82)**\n\nThe catch block around tool extraction is completely silent. Other modules at least use `console.warn`. A single `console.warn('[build] tool extraction failed:', rawPath)` would help diagnose data gaps without breaking the build.\n\n---\n\n## 4. TEST COVERAGE\n\n### 4.1 Overall assessment\n\nCoverage of the pure-logic layer (`stats.ts`, `summarize.ts`, `links.ts`, `search.ts`, `charts.ts`) is comprehensive and well-structured. The integration test in `build.test.ts` covers the happy path thoroughly.\n\n### 4.2 Missing edge cases\n\n**HIGH ÔÇö No test for XSS injection through manifest data**\n\n`renderTranscriptList`, `renderDashboard`, and `renderTranscript` all embed `m.title`, `m.client`, `m.session_id`, `m.cwd`, and `m.branch` into HTML via `escHtml`. There is no test verifying that a manifest with `title: '<script>alert(1)</script>'` produces escaped output. Given that manifests are parsed from JSON files that could be tampered with, this is a meaningful security boundary that should be tested.\n\n**HIGH ÔÇö No test for the inline JS injection path in `renderSearchPage`**\n\n`renderSearchPage` embeds `JSON.stringify(index)` directly into a `<script>` tag (`const SEARCH_INDEX = ${indexJson}`). There is a test that the embedded JSON parses correctly (`search.test.ts` line 110), but no test verifying that an entry containing `</script>` in the title or snippet does not break out of the script context. For example, a title of `foo</script><script>alert(1)</script>` would produce a broken/malicious page. `JSON.stringify` does NOT escape `</script>` sequences. This is a real XSS vector.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö No test for `renderMessageBody` / `applyInlineMarkdown` in `transcript.ts`**\n\nThe inline markdown renderer is not tested at all. Edge cases that could produce incorrect HTML include: nested emphasis (`**bold *and italic* bold**`), backtick-in-bold, and the handling of single `\\n` vs. `\\n\\n` (the current implementation only creates paragraphs on `\\n\\n`, single newlines become `<br>` inside `applyInlineMarkdown` but are treated as paragraph content in `renderInlineText`). There is also no test for the code-block regex behaviour with unterminated backticks.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö No test for `markdownToHtml` in `markdown.ts`**\n\nThe custom markdown converter handles headings, lists, code blocks, YAML front-matter, and inline formatting. There are no unit tests for any of these paths. Edge cases: YAML front-matter only skipped at `i === 0`, so a second `---` block would not be treated as front matter; nested lists are not supported (the `olMatch`/`ulMatch` regexes ignore indentation); the horizontal-rule handler fires for `---` which conflicts with YAML closing delimiter detection.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö `buildReport` test does not verify transcript HTML content**\n\nThe integration test (`build.test.ts` line 189) only asserts that an HTML file exists. It does not assert that the transcript HTML contains the expected message text, the session title, or that `escHtml` was applied. A regression in rendering would not be caught.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `buildDayBuckets` / `buildWeekBuckets` do not test the ISO week boundary correctness**\n\n`isoWeek` in `stats.ts` implements an ISO-8601 week calculation (Thursday-anchor). The test for `buildWeekBuckets` only checks the format regex (`/^\\d{4}-W\\d{2}$/`) and the total count. It does not verify the actual week number for a known date, so an off-by-one in the calculation would pass.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `buildSearchIndex` budget logic is tested loosely**\n\nThe test verifies that `json.length <= 51_000` with 100 sessions each having 200-char snippets. This passes comfortably, but the boundary case ÔÇö where a single entry is so large that `remaining < 20` truncates to nothing ÔÇö is not tested.\n\n---\n\n## 5. DEAD CODE / REDUNDANCY\n\n**HIGH ÔÇö `transcriptHref` is duplicated in three files**\n\nThe function that computes a relative URL from a `ManifestEntry` to its HTML file is independently implemented in:\n\n- `dashboard.ts` line 132ÔÇô136\n- `transcript-list.ts` line 68ÔÇô72\n- `transcript.ts` line 161ÔÇô166\n\nThe three versions are slightly different (root-relative vs. one-level-up vs. two-levels-up from the caller's location). This is the most significant maintenance hazard in the module ÔÇö a future change to the output path structure would require finding and updating all three. This logic belongs in a single exported function in `layout.ts` that accepts the caller's depth as a parameter, or in a dedicated `render/utils.ts`.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö `GoalFile` and `KnowledgeFile` interfaces are identical**\n\nBoth `goals.ts` and `knowledge.ts` define:\n```ts\nexport interface { filename: string; title: string; content: string }\n```\nThese are separate named types, which is fine conceptually, but they are structurally identical and both modules implement an identical `slugify` function. A shared `MarkdownFile` interface and a shared `slugify` utility would eliminate the duplication.\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö `build.ts` `loadMarkdownFiles` duplicates what `extractMarkdownTitle` already does in a slightly different location**\n\n`loadMarkdownFiles` (line 336) and `extractMarkdownTitle` (line 362) are private helpers in `build.ts` that serve knowledge/goals page generation. This logic is entirely unrelated to the build orchestration concern of `build.ts` and should not be there at all ÔÇö it should be co-located with `goals.ts` and `knowledge.ts`, or in a shared util.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `pdf: false` is passed to `buildReport` by `cmd-report.ts` but `BuildReportOptions.pdf` is never read**\n\n`BuildReportOptions` has a `pdf?: boolean` field defined in `types/report.ts`. `buildReport` in `build.ts` accepts it in the options type but never reads it. `cmd-report.ts` always passes `pdf: false`. The `pdf` flag appears to be a placeholder for a feature not yet implemented, but it adds noise to the public interface and could mislead callers into expecting PDF output.\n\n---\n\n## 6. PERFORMANCE\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö Unbounded `Promise.all` parallelism with a polling semaphore**\n\n`build.ts` launches one promise per manifest (line 70ÔÇô105) and calls `Promise.all` on all of them. The concurrency limit of 5 is enforced in `summarize.ts` via busy-polling (`setTimeout(resolve, 100)` in a while loop). This means:\n\n1. All file I/O (reading clean markdown, resolving raw paths, writing HTML) runs unbounded in parallel. For a repository with thousands of transcripts, this will exhaust Node.js file descriptor limits and produce contention.\n2. The 100ms polling interval introduces latency proportional to concurrency contention ÔÇö if all 5 slots are busy, new tasks wait up to 100ms to start.\n\nA proper solution would use a streaming processor (batches of N at a time) that gates both I/O and AI calls behind the semaphore, rather than scattering the gate only around the AI call.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `manifests.find()` inside a `.map()` in `build.ts` (line 96ÔÇô99)**\n\n```ts\nconst backlinkManifests = backlinkIds\n  .map(id => manifests.find(x => x.session_id === id))\n```\n\nThis is O(N ├Ś M) where N = backlink count and M = total manifests. For large datasets this should be a pre-built lookup map (`Map<string, ManifestEntry>`). The same pattern appears to be intended in `links.ts` (where `lookup` is already a plain object), but `build.ts` does not share or reuse it.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `buildSearchIndex` calls `JSON.stringify(entry)` twice per entry (lines 33, 41)**\n\nThe size check serializes the entry to measure bytes, then serializes it again after the potential truncation. One of these could be eliminated.\n\n---\n\n## 7. MAINTAINABILITY\n\n**MEDIUM ÔÇö Inline JavaScript embedded in a TypeScript template string (`search.ts` lines 85ÔÇô138)**\n\nThe vanilla JS search logic is a 50-line string inside `renderSearchPage`. It has its own `escHtml` function that duplicates the one in `layout.ts`. This code is not type-checked, not linted, and not directly testable in isolation. The tests for it only verify substring presence. A change to the search logic requires editing a template string ÔÇö no autocomplete, no type errors. This should be extracted to a static `.js` asset file bundled/inlined at build time, or at minimum the function-level duplication (`escHtml`) should be eliminated.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `isoWeek` implementation is duplicated between `stats.ts` and `charts.ts`**\n\n`isoWeek` (in `stats.ts`, line 134) and `toIsoWeek` (in `charts.ts`, line 263) implement the same ISO week calculation with identical logic. They are separate private functions. The canonical version should be exported from `stats.ts` and imported by `charts.ts`.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö No type guard on parsed manifest data**\n\n`loadManifestsRecursive` in `build.ts` performs a type assertion (`as ManifestEntry`) after checking only `session_id` and `client`. A manifest missing `message_count` or `started_at` would pass the guard and cause silent arithmetic errors downstream (NaN in stats, empty date ranges). A schema validation step (even a manual field check) would catch malformed manifests early.\n\n---\n\n## 8. PLAN COMPLIANCE\n\n**CRITICAL ÔÇö Cache location deviates from spec**\n\nThe plan spec (and the `references/transcript-archive.md` global configuration document) places the global search index and derived artifacts at `~/.memorytree/`. The cache for AI summaries is placed at `join(output, '.cache')` ÔÇö inside `Memory/07_reports/.cache/`. This means:\n\n- The cache is **inside the output directory**, which is gitignored as `Memory/07_reports/`. The cache is therefore gitignored and lost every time the output directory is cleaned.\n- If the user specifies a custom `--output`, the cache follows it rather than being stable across output changes.\n\nThe spec implies the cache should be stable and persistent, decoupled from the ephemeral output. It should live at a path like `join(root, 'Memory', '07_reports', '.cache')` that is stable regardless of the `--output` flag, or at a user-level path like `~/.memorytree/report-cache/`. As currently written, re-running `report build` after `rm -rf Memory/07_reports` will re-issue API calls for every transcript.\n\n**HIGH ÔÇö Model ID used as default does not match the plan's stated model**\n\n`build.ts` line 32 and `main.ts` line 177 both reference `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`. The performance guidelines specify `claude-haiku-4-5` as the appropriate lightweight model for worker agents. The date-stamped model ID `20251001` is an internal version that may be deprecated. The CLI `--model` default in `main.ts` and the `DEFAULT_MODEL` constant in `build.ts` must be kept in sync manually ÔÇö if one changes, the other becomes stale. These should reference a single constant defined once.\n\n**LOW ÔÇö `pdf?: boolean` in `BuildReportOptions` is exposed in the type but never implemented**\n\nIf the plan included a PDF output feature, it is not present. If it was descoped, the option should be removed from the interface to avoid confusion.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File(s) | Issue |\n|---|----------|---------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `build.ts` | Cache placed inside gitignored output dir; lost on clean build |\n| 2 | HIGH | `search.ts` | `</script>` injection not escaped in inline JSON ÔÇö real XSS vector |\n| 3 | HIGH | `links.ts` | Arrays mutated in-place, violating immutability policy |\n| 4 | HIGH | `build.ts`, `dashboard.ts`, `transcript-list.ts`, `transcript.ts` | `transcriptHref` triplicated with slightly different logic |\n| 5 | HIGH | Tests | No XSS/escaping test for manifest-derived HTML content |\n| 6 | HIGH | `build.ts` | Default model constant not aligned with CLI flag default ÔÇö dual maintenance risk |\n| 7 | MEDIUM | `summarize.ts` | Module-level mutable `activeCalls` counter; polling semaphore |\n| 8 | MEDIUM | `build.ts` | File I/O parallelism is unbounded; only AI calls are gated |\n| 9 | MEDIUM | `build.ts` | `loadMarkdownFiles`, `extractMarkdownTitle` live in orchestrator, not renderer |\n| 10 | MEDIUM | `goals.ts`, `knowledge.ts` | Identical `GoalFile`/`KnowledgeFile` interfaces and `slugify` duplicated |\n| 11 | MEDIUM | Tests | No unit tests for `markdownToHtml` or `renderMessageBody` |\n| 12 | MEDIUM | `search.ts` | Inline JS string with duplicated `escHtml`; not type-checked or linted |\n| 13 | MEDIUM | `build.ts` | O(N├ŚM) backlink lookup should use a pre-built `Map` |\n| 14 | LOW | `dashboard.ts` | `clientBadge` exported from page renderer, imported by sibling pages |\n| 15 | LOW | `stats.ts`, `charts.ts` | `isoWeek`/`toIsoWeek` duplicated |\n| 16 | LOW | `build.ts` | Silent catch in tool extraction; no warning logged |\n| 17 | LOW | Tests | No test verifying transcript HTML content (only file existence) |\n| 18 | LOW | `types/report.ts` | `pdf?: boolean` declared but never read; vestigial |\n| 19 | LOW | `build.ts` | No type guard on manifest fields beyond `session_id`+`client` |\n| 20 | LOW | Tests | ISO week calculation not verified against known dates |\n\n---\n\n## Key Recommendations (priority order)\n\n1. **Fix the `</script>` injection** in `renderSearchPage` ÔÇö replace the raw `JSON.stringify` embed with a version that escapes `</` sequences (e.g., replace `</` with `<\\/` inside the JSON string before embedding).\n\n2. **Move the summary cache** to a stable path outside the output directory, such as `join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache')`, so it survives `rm -rf Memory/07_reports`.\n\n3. **Extract `transcriptHref` into a single shared function** in `layout.ts` parameterized by caller depth, and delete the three copies.\n\n4. **Fix `links.ts` mutation** ÔÇö replace the `.push()` pattern with an immutable array update to comply with the project's own coding standard.\n\n5. **Add a unit test module for `markdown.ts` and `transcript.ts` render functions**, including at least one XSS injection case for manifest-controlled fields.\n\n6. **Consolidate the default model constant** into `types/report.ts` or a `constants.ts` file so `build.ts` and `main.ts` share the same reference.",
    "type": "text"
  },
  {

]

Fix the markdown.ts link rendering (C4) ÔÇö process links before HTML-escaping:

Tool Call #204 Edit toolu_01TtFCHPQWH8qQzrv766eJ13 10:23:50
Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/markdown.ts","new_string":"function applyInline(text: string): string {\n // Process links before H...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/markdown.ts",
  "old_string": "function applyInline(text: string): string {\n  const escaped = escHtml(text)\n  return escaped\n    .replace(/\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n    .replace(/\\*(.+?)\\*/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n    .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n    .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n    .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '<code>$1</code>')\n    .replace(/\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {\n      return `<a href=\"${escHtml(href)}\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">${label}</a>`\n    })\n}",
  "new_string": "function applyInline(text: string): string {\n  // Process links before HTML-escaping to avoid double-escaping href/label\n  const withLinks = text.replace(/\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {\n    return `<a href=\"${escHtml(href)}\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">${escHtml(label)}</a>`\n  })\n  // Escape remaining text, then apply inline patterns on already-safe content\n  // Split on the <a> tags we just inserted so they are not re-escaped\n  const parts = withLinks.split(/(<a [^>]+>.*?<\\/a>)/g)\n  const processed = parts\n    .map((part, i) => {\n      if (i % 2 === 1) return part // already-generated <a> tag, pass through\n      const escaped = escHtml(part)\n      return escaped\n        .replace(/\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n        .replace(/\\*(.+?)\\*/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n        .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n        .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n        .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '<code>$1</code>')\n    })\n    .join('')\n  return processed\n}"
}

Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-report.ts","new_string":" const server = createServer((req, res) => {\n let urlPath: string\n try {\n url...

  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-report.ts",
  "old_string": "  const server = createServer((req, res) => {\n    const urlPath = (req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/'\n    const safePath = urlPath.replace(/\\.\\./g, '')\n    let filePath = join(root, safePath)\n\n    // Serve index.html for directory requests\n    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {\n      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')\n    }\n\n    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {\n      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })\n      res.end('Not found')\n      return\n    }\n\n    const ext = extname(filePath)\n    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'\n    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })\n    createReadStream(filePath).pipe(res)\n  })",
  "new_string": "  const server = createServer((req, res) => {\n    let urlPath: string\n    try {\n      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')\n    } catch {\n      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })\n      res.end('Bad request')\n      return\n    }\n\n    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)\n\n    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root\n    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + '/') && filePath !== root) {\n      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })\n      res.end('Forbidden')\n      return\n    }\n\n    // Serve index.html for directory requests\n    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {\n      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')\n    }\n\n    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {\n      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })\n      res.end('Not found')\n      return\n    }\n\n    const ext = extname(filePath)\n    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'\n    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })\n    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)\n    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })\n    stream.pipe(res)\n  })"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/layout.ts",
  "old_string": "export function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = ''): string {",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Transcript URL helpers (single canonical implementation)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Compute the stem (filename without .md) for a manifest's clean path.\n * Falls back to session_id.\n */\nexport function manifestStem(m: ManifestEntry): string {\n  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path || m.global_clean_path || ''\n  return cleanPath ? basename(cleanPath, '.md') : m.session_id\n}\n\n/**\n * URL to a transcript page relative to the **root** of the report output dir.\n * Use this from index.html and transcripts/index.html.\n */\nexport function transcriptUrlFromRoot(m: ManifestEntry): string {\n  return `transcripts/${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`\n}\n\n/**\n * URL to a transcript page relative to **another transcript** page.\n * Use this from individual transcript pages.\n */\nexport function transcriptUrlFromTranscript(m: ManifestEntry): string {\n  return `../${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// HTML shell\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = ''): string {"
}

1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/.
     3Ôćĺ */
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺimport { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav } from './layout.js'
     6Ôćĺimport { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9Ôćĺ// Goals page
    10Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺexport interface GoalFile {
    13Ôćĺ  filename: string
    14Ôćĺ  title: string
    15Ôćĺ  content: string
    16Ôćĺ}
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺexport function renderGoals(files: GoalFile[]): string {
    19Ôćĺ  const nav = renderNav('goals', false)
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ  if (files.length === 0) {
    22Ôćĺ    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    23Ôćĺ  <h1>Goals</h1>
    24Ôćĺ  <p class="subtitle">No goal files found in Memory/01_goals/.</p>
    25Ôćĺ</div>`
    26Ôćĺ    return htmlShell('Goals', content, nav)
    27Ôćĺ  }
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ  const sections = files
    30Ôćĺ    .map(f => {
    31Ôćĺ      const htmlContent = markdownToHtml(f.content)
    32Ôćĺ      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugify(f.filename))}">
    33Ôćĺ  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
    34Ôćĺ  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
    35Ôćĺ  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
    36Ôćĺ</div>`
    37Ôćĺ    })
    38Ôćĺ    .join('\n')
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ  const content = `<div class="page-header">
    41Ôćĺ  <h1>Goals</h1>
    42Ôćĺ  <p class="subtitle">${files.length} goal file(s)</p>
    43Ôćĺ</div>
    44Ôćĺ${sections}`
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ  return htmlShell('Goals', content, nav)
    47Ôćĺ}
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺfunction slugify(name: string): string {
    50Ôćĺ  return name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    51Ôćĺ}

Show output payload 
1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * HTML layout helpers: shell, navigation, and HTML escaping.
     3Ôćĺ */
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺimport { basename } from 'node:path'
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     8Ôćĺimport { REPORT_CSS } from './css.js'
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11Ôćĺ// HTML escaping
    12Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺexport function escHtml(text: string): string {
    15Ôćĺ  return text
    16Ôćĺ    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
    17Ôćĺ    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
    18Ôćĺ    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
    19Ôćĺ    .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
    20Ôćĺ    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;')
    21Ôćĺ}
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24Ôćĺ// Navigation
    25Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺexport type NavPage = 'dashboard' | 'transcripts' | 'goals' | 'knowledge' | 'search'
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺconst NAV_ITEMS: Array<{ id: NavPage; label: string; href: string }> = [
    30Ôćĺ  { id: 'dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', href: '../index.html' },
    31Ôćĺ  { id: 'transcripts', label: 'Transcripts', href: '../transcripts/index.html' },
    32Ôćĺ  { id: 'goals', label: 'Goals', href: '../goals/index.html' },
    33Ôćĺ  { id: 'knowledge', label: 'Knowledge', href: '../knowledge/index.html' },
    34Ôćĺ  { id: 'search', label: 'Search', href: '../search.html' },
    35Ôćĺ]
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ/** Nav items for root-level pages (index.html, search.html) use relative paths */
    38Ôćĺconst NAV_ITEMS_ROOT: Array<{ id: NavPage; label: string; href: string }> = [
    39Ôćĺ  { id: 'dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', href: 'index.html' },
    40Ôćĺ  { id: 'transcripts', label: 'Transcripts', href: 'transcripts/index.html' },
    41Ôćĺ  { id: 'goals', label: 'Goals', href: 'goals/index.html' },
    42Ôćĺ  { id: 'knowledge', label: 'Knowledge', href: 'knowledge/index.html' },
    43Ôćĺ  { id: 'search', label: 'Search', href: 'search.html' },
    44Ôćĺ]
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺexport function renderNav(current: NavPage, rootLevel = false): string {
    47Ôćĺ  const items = rootLevel ? NAV_ITEMS_ROOT : NAV_ITEMS
    48Ôćĺ  const links = items
    49Ôćĺ    .map(item => {
    50Ôćĺ      const cls = item.id === current ? 'nav-link active' : 'nav-link'
    51Ôćĺ      return `<a href="${item.href}" class="${cls}">${item.label}</a>`
    52Ôćĺ    })
    53Ôćĺ    .join('')
    54Ôćĺ  return `<nav class="nav"><span class="nav-brand">MemoryTree</span>${links}</nav>`
    55Ôćĺ}
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58Ôćĺ// HTML shell
    59Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62Ôćĺ// Transcript URL helpers (single canonical implementation)
    63Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ/**
    66Ôćĺ * Compute the stem (filename without .md) for a manifest's clean path.
    67Ôćĺ * Falls back to session_id.
    68Ôćĺ */
    69Ôćĺexport function manifestStem(m: ManifestEntry): string {
    70Ôćĺ  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path || m.global_clean_path || ''
    71Ôćĺ  return cleanPath ? basename(cleanPath, '.md') : m.session_id
    72Ôćĺ}
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ/**
    75Ôćĺ * URL to a transcript page relative to the **root** of the report output dir.
    76Ôćĺ * Use this from index.html and transcripts/index.html.
    77Ôćĺ */
    78Ôćĺexport function transcriptUrlFromRoot(m: ManifestEntry): string {
    79Ôćĺ  return `transcripts/${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
    80Ôćĺ}
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ/**
    83Ôćĺ * URL to a transcript page relative to **another transcript** page.
    84Ôćĺ * Use this from individual transcript pages.
    85Ôćĺ */
    86Ôćĺexport function transcriptUrlFromTranscript(m: ManifestEntry): string {
    87Ôćĺ  return `../${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
    88Ôćĺ}
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91Ôćĺ// HTML shell
    92Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺexport function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = ''): string {
    95Ôćĺ  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
    96Ôćĺ<html lang="en">
    97Ôćĺ<head>
    98Ôćĺ<meta charset="UTF-8">
    99Ôćĺ<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
   100Ôćĺ<title>${escHtml(title)} ÔÇö MemoryTree</title>
   101Ôćĺ<style>${REPORT_CSS}</style>
   102Ôćĺ${extraHead}
   103Ôćĺ</head>
   104Ôćĺ<body>
   105Ôćĺ${nav}
   106Ôćĺ<div class="container">
   107Ôćĺ${content}
   108Ôćĺ</div>
   109Ôćĺ</body>
   110Ôćĺ</html>`
   111Ôćĺ}
   112Ôćĺ

Tool Call #257 Edit toolu_01Mh3RjaAAzFVZ5ASmaVjazj 10:28:47
Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/stats.ts","new_string":"/** Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week string from an ISO timestamp. */\nfunction isoWeek...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/stats.ts",
  "old_string": "/** Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week string from an ISO timestamp. */\nfunction isoWeek(ts: string): string {\n  if (!ts) return ''\n  try {\n    const d = new Date(ts)\n    if (isNaN(d.getTime())) return ''\n    // ISO week: Thursday determines the week number\n    const thursday = new Date(d)\n    thursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))\n    const year = thursday.getFullYear()\n    const jan4 = new Date(year, 0, 4)\n    const week = Math.ceil(\n      ((thursday.getTime() - jan4.getTime()) / 86400000 + jan4.getDay() + 1) / 7,\n    )\n    return `${year}-W${String(week).padStart(2, '0')}`\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}",
  "new_string": "/** Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week string from an ISO timestamp. */\nfunction isoWeek(ts: string): string {\n  if (!ts) return ''\n  try {\n    const d = new Date(ts)\n    if (isNaN(d.getTime())) return ''\n    return isoWeekKey(d)\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week key from a Date object.\n * Exported so chart renderers can reuse without duplicating the formula.\n */\nexport function isoWeekKey(d: Date): string {\n  // ISO week: the week containing Thursday; Monday = week start\n  const thursday = new Date(d)\n  thursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))\n  const year = thursday.getFullYear()\n  const jan4 = new Date(year, 0, 4)\n  const week = Math.ceil(\n    ((thursday.getTime() - jan4.getTime()) / 86400000 + jan4.getDay() + 1) / 7,\n  )\n  return `${year}-W${String(week).padStart(2, '0')}`\n}"
}

{

  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/charts.ts",
  "old_string": "function getLast52Weeks(): string[] {\n  const weeks: string[] = []\n  const today = new Date()\n  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)\n  for (let i = 51; i >= 0; i--) {\n    const d = new Date(today)\n    d.setDate(d.getDate() - i * 7)\n    weeks.push(toIsoWeek(d))\n  }\n  return weeks\n}\n\nfunction toIsoWeek(d: Date): string {\n  const thursday = new Date(d)\n  thursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))\n  const year = thursday.getFullYear()\n  const jan4 = new Date(year, 0, 4)\n  const week = Math.ceil(\n    ((thursday.getTime() - jan4.getTime()) / 86400000 + jan4.getDay() + 1) / 7,\n  )\n  return `${year}-W${String(week).padStart(2, '0')}`\n}",
  "new_string": "function getLast52Weeks(): string[] {\n  const weeks: string[] = []\n  const today = new Date()\n  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)\n  for (let i = 51; i >= 0; i--) {\n    const d = new Date(today)\n    d.setDate(d.getDate() - i * 7)\n    weeks.push(isoWeekKey(d))\n  }\n  return weeks\n}"
}

  "old_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// HTML shell\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = ''): string {",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Client badge (allowlisted CSS class)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst KNOWN_CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])\n\n/** Render a coloured client badge. CSS class is allowlisted to prevent injection. */\nexport function clientBadge(client: string): string {\n  const safeClass = KNOWN_CLIENTS.has(client) ? client : 'unknown'\n  return `<span class=\"badge badge-${safeClass}\">${escHtml(client)}</span>`\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared MarkdownFile type and slug helper\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface MarkdownFile {\n  filename: string\n  title: string\n  content: string\n}\n\nexport function slugifyName(name: string): string {\n  return name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// HTML shell\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = ''): string {"
}

  "content": "/**\n * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/.\n */\n\nimport { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'\nimport type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'\nimport { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'\n\nexport type GoalFile = MarkdownFile\n\nexport function renderGoals(files: GoalFile[]): string {\n  const nav = renderNav('goals', false)\n\n  if (files.length === 0) {\n    const content = `<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>Goals</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">No goal files found in Memory/01_goals/.</p>\n</div>`\n    return htmlShell('Goals', content, nav)\n  }\n\n  const sections = files\n    .map(f => {\n      const htmlContent = markdownToHtml(f.content)\n      return `<div class=\"card\" id=\"${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}\">\n  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>\n  <p style=\"font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem\">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>\n  <div class=\"markdown-body\">${htmlContent}</div>\n</div>`\n    })\n    .join('\\n')\n\n  const content = `<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>Goals</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">${files.length} goal file(s)</p>\n</div>\n${sections}`\n\n  return htmlShell('Goals', content, nav)\n}\n"
}

Expand full message

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
- Primary: Implement the full memorytree report plan ÔÇö a self-contained multi-page HTML website generated from the MemoryTree workspace (transcripts, goals, knowledge) with inline SVG charts, AI session summaries (cached by sha256), full-text search, and heartbeat daemon integration.
- Secondary: After implementation, the user asked "ÚâŻňĚ▓š╗Ćň«îŠłÉń║ćňÉŚ ŠîëŔ«íňłĺ" (confirm all done per plan), then requested "review 2ŠČí" (two separate code reviews), then requested to fix all CRITICAL/HIGH issues, then requested "ÚťÇŔŽüňĄäšÉćlowšÜäÚŚ«Úóś´╝îňÉîŠŚÂńŻáňĹŐŔ»ëŠłĹšÄ░ňťĘŠłĹń╗ČŠâ│šÜäŠÇ╗šŻĹÚíÁňť░ňŁÇ ŠłĹšé╣ňç╗ŠčąšťőńŞÇńŞő" (fix LOW issues + provide the URL to view the report).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- TypeScript NodeNext ESM with strict mode, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes ÔÇö all imports need .js extension
- tsup build tool, Vitest test framework
- Inline SVG charts: GitHub-style activity heatmap (53├Ś7 rect grid), doughnut (stroke-dasharray circles), weekly line (polyline + filled path), horizontal bar chart ÔÇö zero external dependencies
- GitHub Dark CSS palette with CSS variables ÔÇö all self-contained, works from file://
- AI Summary: dynamic import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') with try/catch for MODULE_NOT_FOUND; sha256-keyed JSON cache; max-5 concurrent semaphore (module-level counter + setTimeout polling)
- HTML escaping: escHtml() must be applied to ALL user-controlled content; JSON.stringify does NOT escape </script> (requires .replace(/<\//g, '<\\/'))
- Immutability principle: all array/object updates must return new copies, never .push() in-place
- Clean markdown format: ## Messages section with ### N. role\n- Timestamp: \ISO\\n\ntext per message; tool events NOT stored in clean markdown
- Path safety: join(root, manifest.repo_clean_path) for repo paths; manifest.global_clean_path as fallback
- Cache stability: cache must live at Memory/.report-cache/ (outside gitignored Memory/07_reports/), not inside output dir

3. Files and Code Sections:

- src/types/report.ts (new) ÔÇö Core type definitions

     export interface ReportStats {
       totalSessions: number; totalMessages: number; totalToolEvents: number
       activeDays: number; dateRange: { from: string; to: string }
       clientCounts: Record<string, number>; dayBuckets: Record<string, number>
       weekBuckets: Record<string, number>; toolCounts: Record<string, number>
     }
     export interface SummaryCache { sha256: string; summary: string; generated_at: string }
     export interface LinkGraph {
       backlinks: Record<string, string[]>; forwardLinks: Record<string, string[]>
     }
     export interface SearchIndexEntry { url: string; title: string; client: string; date: string; snippet: string }
     export interface BuildReportOptions { root: string; output: string; noAi?: boolean; model?: string }
     // Note: pdf?: boolean was removed (vestigial, never implemented)

- src/report/render/layout.ts (new, then heavily modified) ÔÇö Core HTML utilities; canonical home for shared helpers
- Contains: escHtml(), renderNav() (with escHtml on labels/hrefs), htmlShell(), manifestStem(), transcriptUrlFromRoot(), transcriptUrlFromTranscript(), clientBadge() (moved from dashboard.ts, with CSS class allowlist), MarkdownFile interface, slugifyName()

     const KNOWN_CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])
     export function clientBadge(client: string): string {
       const safeClass = KNOWN_CLIENTS.has(client) ? client : 'unknown'
       return `<span class="badge badge-${safeClass}">${escHtml(client)}</span>`
     }
     export function transcriptUrlFromRoot(m: ManifestEntry): string {
       return `transcripts/${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
     }
     export function transcriptUrlFromTranscript(m: ManifestEntry): string {
       return `../${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
     }
     export interface MarkdownFile { filename: string; title: string; content: string }
     export function slugifyName(name: string): string {
       return name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
     }

- src/report/render/search.ts (new) ÔÇö Search page with embedded JSON index
- Critical fix: escape </script> in serialized JSON

     const indexJson = JSON.stringify(index).replace(/<\//g, '<\\/')

- Highlight uses manual substring loop (not regex) to avoid esbuild parsing issues with complex regex in template strings

- src/report/render/links.ts (new) ÔÇö Link graph builder
- Fixed: replaced .push() mutation with immutable spread updates

     const fwd = forwardLinks[m.session_id] ?? []
     if (!fwd.includes(targetId)) {
       forwardLinks[m.session_id] = [...fwd, targetId]
     }
     const back = backlinks[targetId] ?? []
     if (!back.includes(m.session_id)) {
       backlinks[targetId] = [...back, m.session_id]
     }

- src/report/render/charts.ts (new) ÔÇö All inline SVG charts
- Fixed division-by-zero in line chart: data.length > 1 ? padL + (i / (data.length - 1)) * plotW : padL + plotW / 2
- Removed dead / 1 from doughnut dashoffset: (-offset * circumference).toFixed(2)
- Heatmap: leftPad changed from 0 to 28 so day labels (Mon/Wed/Fri) are visible; loop simplified to while (weeks.length < 53)
- Day label x: leftPad - 4 (was hardcoded -2 outside viewBox)
- Now imports isoWeekKey from ../stats.js instead of duplicating the formula

- src/report/stats.ts (new) ÔÇö Pure statistics computation
- Exports isoWeekKey(d: Date): string (extracted from private isoWeek) so charts.ts can reuse without duplication

     export function isoWeekKey(d: Date): string {
       const thursday = new Date(d)
       thursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))
       const year = thursday.getFullYear()
       const jan4 = new Date(year, 0, 4)
       const week = Math.ceil(
         ((thursday.getTime() - jan4.getTime()) / 86400000 + jan4.getDay() + 1) / 7,
       )
       return `${year}-W${String(week).padStart(2, '0')}`
     }

- src/report/render/markdown.ts (new) ÔÇö Minimal MarkdownÔćĺHTML converter (no external lib)
- Fixed link rendering: process [label](href) BEFORE escHtml() to avoid double-escaping URLs

     function applyInline(text: string): string {
       const withLinks = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, (_, label, href) => {
         return `<a href="${escHtml(href)}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">${escHtml(label)}</a>`
       })
       const parts = withLinks.split(/(<a [^>]+>.*?<\/a>)/g)
       return parts.map((part, i) => {
         if (i % 2 === 1) return part // pass-through generated <a> tag
         const escaped = escHtml(part)
         return escaped
           .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')
           // ... other inline patterns
       }).join('')
     }

- src/report/render/goals.ts (new, then updated) ÔÇö Goals page
- Now uses MarkdownFile and slugifyName from layout.ts instead of duplicating them

     import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
     import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
     export type GoalFile = MarkdownFile

- src/report/render/knowledge.ts (new) ÔÇö Knowledge page; same pattern as goals.ts (pending update to use MarkdownFile)

- src/report/render/dashboard.ts (new) ÔÇö Dashboard: stat cards + charts + recent sessions
- clientBadge moved out (now imported from layout.ts)

- src/report/render/transcript-list.ts (new) ÔÇö Session list; imports clientBadge from layout.ts

- src/report/render/transcript.ts (new) ÔÇö Individual session page with messages, summary, backlinks
- Imports clientBadge and transcriptUrlFromTranscript from layout.ts

- src/report/summarize.ts (new) ÔÇö AI summary with caching
- Cache at join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache') (stable, outside gitignored output)
- Dynamic import with catch for MODULE_NOT_FOUND
- Semaphore: let activeCalls = 0; const MAX_CONCURRENT = 5

- src/report/build.ts (new) ÔÇö Main orchestrator

     const cacheDir = join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache') // fixed: outside gitignored dir

- parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content) exported for tests; searches for \n## Messages in content
- ensureGitignore(root, 'Memory/07_reports/') called at start
- Loads manifests recursively from Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/
- Processes one transcript at a time; spawns promises for parallel summarization (bounded by semaphore)

- src/cli/cmd-report.ts (new) ÔÇö CLI adapter
- Serve command: added decodeURIComponent, startsWith(root) containment check, stream.on('error', () => res.destroy())

- src/cli/main.ts (modified) ÔÇö Added report build and report serve subcommands
- --no-ai flag: Commander sets opts.ai = false; mapped as noAi: opts.ai === false

- src/heartbeat/config.ts (modified) ÔÇö Added fields:

     readonly generate_report: boolean  // default: false
     readonly ai_summary_model: string  // default: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'

- src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts (modified) ÔÇö Calls buildReport() after gitCommitAndPush if config.generate_report:

     if (config.generate_report) {
       try {
         const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
         await buildReport({ root: projectPath, output: join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports'),
           noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'], model: config.ai_summary_model, pdf: false })
         logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated.`)
       } catch (err) { logger.warn(...) /* never propagate */ }
     }

- package.json (modified) ÔÇö Added "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0"

- Test files (7 new):
- tests/report/stats.test.ts, tests/report/render/charts.test.ts, tests/report/render/links.test.ts, tests/report/render/search.test.ts, tests/report/summarize.test.ts, tests/report/build.test.ts
- tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts (updated: added new fields to all cfg objects, added new describe block for generate_report/ai_summary_model)

4. Errors and Fixes:
- Build error: regex in template string ÔÇö search.ts had complex regex /[.*+?^${}()|[\\]\\\\]/g inside inline JS template literal, confusing esbuild parser. Fixed by replacing regex-based highlighting with a manual character-by-character substring loop using indexOf.
- Test error: await in non-async function ÔÇö summarize.test.ts line 94 used await import(...) inside a sync it(). Fixed by adding async to the test callback.
- Test error: parseMessagesFromMarkdown returned empty ÔÇö test markdown '## Messages\n...' lacked leading \n, but the parser searches for '\n## Messages'. Fixed by prepending \n to the test string: ` \n## Messages\n\n### 1. user...
- Test error: require() in ESM ÔÇö build.test.ts used require('node:fs').readFileSync(...). Fixed by adding readFileSync to the top-level import and removing the dynamic require calls.
- CRITICAL XSS: </script> injection ÔÇö JSON.stringify doesn't escape </script>, enabling script injection via manifest-controlled titles/snippets. Fixed: .replace(/<\//g, '<\\/') after JSON.stringify.
- CRITICAL immutability violation: links.ts ÔÇö Used .push() on stored arrays. Fixed with [...existing, id] immutable updates.
- CRITICAL: Cache in gitignored dir ÔÇö cacheDir = join(output, '.cache') lost on rm -rf. Fixed: cacheDir = join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache').
- CRITICAL: Markdown link double-escaping ÔÇö applyInline called escHtml on entire text first, then regex matched [label](href) ÔÇö but &quot; in hrefs broke URL matching and caused double-encoding. Fixed by processing link regex before escHtml, splitting on generated <a> tags.
- HIGH: Division by zero ÔÇö Line chart with data.length === 1: i / (data.length - 1) = 0/0 = NaN. Fixed with guard: data.length > 1 ? ... : padL + plotW / 2.
- HIGH: Path traversal in serve ÔÇö No root containment check. Fixed: decodeURIComponent + if (!filePath.startsWith(root + '/') && filePath !== root) Ôćĺ 403.
- HIGH: Stream pipe unhandled error ÔÇö createReadStream.pipe(res) crashed server on missing file. Fixed: stream.on('error', () => res.destroy()).
- HIGH: transcriptHref triplicated ÔÇö Three slightly different implementations in dashboard.ts, transcript-list.ts, transcript.ts. Fixed: single transcriptUrlFromRoot / transcriptUrlFromTranscript` in layout.ts.

5. Problem Solving:
- Designed memory-efficient build pipeline: one manifest at a time, promises collected but I/O sequential per transcript
- Used Promise.all with bounded semaphore (max 5) for AI calls
- Handled NodeNext ESM .js extension requirement throughout
- Strict TypeScript: noUncheckedIndexedAccess handled with ?? fallback patterns everywhere; exactOptionalPropertyTypes respected
- Clean markdown parsing without re-running expensive full transcript parser
- SVG arc math for doughnut: stroke-dasharray + stroke-dashoffset approach (simpler than arc paths)
- ISO week calculation verified algebraically correct despite unconventional expression

6. All User Messages:
- "Implement the following plan: [full memorytree report plan with architecture, output structure, pages, charts, AI summary, config, heartbeat, tests, verification, implementation order]"
- "ÚâŻiňĚ▓š╗Ćň«îŠłÉń║ćňÉŚ ŠîëŔ«íňłĺ" (Has everything been completed according to the plan?)

8. Current Work:
Immediately before the summary request, LOW issues were being fixed. The following had been completed:
- L1 (isoWeek deduplication): isoWeekKey(d: Date) exported from stats.ts; charts.ts now imports it instead of duplicating
- L2 (clientBadge location): Moved from dashboard.ts to layout.ts with CSS class allowlist; all three import sites updated
- L4 (renderNav escHtml): Added escHtml to nav item hrefs and labels
- L9 (dead / 1): Removed from doughnut dashoffset expression
- M6/L8 (heatmap loop + leftPad): Loop simplified to while (weeks.length < 53); leftPad changed from 0 to 28; day label x uses leftPad - 4
- GoalFile/KnowledgeFile deduplication: goals.ts updated to use MarkdownFile + slugifyName from layout.ts; knowledge.ts update was PENDING at time of summary

Last completed edit was goals.ts:

   import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
   import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
   export type GoalFile = MarkdownFile

    11Ôćĺ  clientCounts: Record<string, number>

    13Ôćĺ  dayBuckets: Record<string, number>
    14Ôćĺ  /** 'YYYY-WNN' Ôćĺ message count */
    15Ôćĺ  weekBuckets: Record<string, number>

    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺexport interface SummaryCache {

    24Ôćĺ}
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺexport interface LinkGraph {

1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * Knowledge page: renders markdown files from Memory/04_knowledge/.
     3Ôćĺ */
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺimport { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav } from './layout.js'
     6Ôćĺimport { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9Ôćĺ// Knowledge page
    10Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺexport interface KnowledgeFile {
    13Ôćĺ  filename: string
    14Ôćĺ  title: string
    15Ôćĺ  content: string
    16Ôćĺ}
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺexport function renderKnowledge(files: KnowledgeFile[]): string {
    19Ôćĺ  const nav = renderNav('knowledge', false)
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ  const subtitle = `${files.length} knowledge file(s)`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ  if (files.length === 0) {
    24Ôćĺ    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    25Ôćĺ  <h1>Knowledge</h1>
    26Ôćĺ  <p class="subtitle">No knowledge files found in Memory/04_knowledge/.</p>
    27Ôćĺ</div>`
    28Ôćĺ    return htmlShell('Knowledge', content, nav)
    29Ôćĺ  }
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ  const sections = files
    32Ôćĺ    .map(f => {
    33Ôćĺ      const htmlContent = markdownToHtml(f.content)
    34Ôćĺ      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugify(f.filename))}">
    35Ôćĺ  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
    36Ôćĺ  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
    37Ôćĺ  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
    38Ôćĺ</div>`
    39Ôćĺ    })
    40Ôćĺ    .join('\n')
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ  const content = `<div class="page-header">
    43Ôćĺ  <h1>Knowledge</h1>
    44Ôćĺ  <p class="subtitle">${subtitle}</p>
    45Ôćĺ</div>
    46Ôćĺ${sections}`
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ  return htmlShell('Knowledge', content, nav)
    49Ôćĺ}
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺfunction slugify(name: string): string {
    52Ôćĺ  return name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    53Ôćĺ}
    54Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * GitHub Dark-inspired CSS for the report website.
     3Ôćĺ * Self-contained ÔÇö no external CDN dependencies.
     4Ôćĺ */
     5Ôćĺ
     6Ôćĺexport const REPORT_CSS = `
     7Ôćĺ:root {
     8Ôćĺ  --bg: #0d1117;
     9Ôćĺ  --bg-secondary: #161b22;
    10Ôćĺ  --bg-card: #21262d;
    11Ôćĺ  --border: #30363d;
    12Ôćĺ  --text: #e6edf3;
    13Ôćĺ  --text-muted: #8b949e;
    14Ôćĺ  --accent: #58a6ff;
    15Ôćĺ  --accent-hover: #79b8ff;
    16Ôćĺ  --green-0: #161b22;
    17Ôćĺ  --green-1: #0e4429;
    18Ôćĺ  --green-2: #006d32;
    19Ôćĺ  --green-3: #26a641;
    20Ôćĺ  --green-4: #39d353;
    21Ôćĺ  --user-bg: #1c2d3e;
    22Ôćĺ  --user-border: #388bfd44;
    23Ôćĺ  --assistant-bg: #1c1c1c;
    24Ôćĺ  --assistant-border: #30363d;
    25Ôćĺ  --code-bg: #161b22;
    26Ôćĺ  --badge-codex: #1f6feb;
    27Ôćĺ  --badge-claude: #6e40c9;
    28Ôćĺ  --badge-gemini: #1a7f37;
    29Ôćĺ  --danger: #f85149;
    30Ôćĺ  --warning: #d29922;
    31Ôćĺ  --success: #3fb950;
    32Ôćĺ  --font-mono: 'SF Mono', 'Consolas', 'Liberation Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;
    33Ôćĺ}
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺhtml { font-size: 16px; scroll-behavior: smooth; }
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺbody {
    40Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg);
    41Ôćĺ  color: var(--text);
    42Ôćĺ  font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;
    43Ôćĺ  line-height: 1.6;
    44Ôćĺ  min-height: 100vh;
    45Ôćĺ}
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺa { color: var(--accent); text-decoration: none; }
    48Ôćĺa:hover { color: var(--accent-hover); text-decoration: underline; }
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺcode {
    51Ôćĺ  font-family: var(--font-mono);
    52Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.875em;
    53Ôćĺ  background: var(--code-bg);
    54Ôćĺ  padding: 0.15em 0.4em;
    55Ôćĺ  border-radius: 4px;
    56Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
    57Ôćĺ}
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺpre {
    60Ôćĺ  background: var(--code-bg);
    61Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
    62Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
    63Ôćĺ  padding: 1rem;
    64Ôćĺ  overflow-x: auto;
    65Ôćĺ  margin: 0.75rem 0;
    66Ôćĺ}
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺpre code {
    69Ôćĺ  background: none;
    70Ôćĺ  border: none;
    71Ôćĺ  padding: 0;
    72Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.875rem;
    73Ôćĺ}
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺh1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
    76Ôćĺ  color: var(--text);
    77Ôćĺ  line-height: 1.3;
    78Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
    79Ôćĺ}
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺh1 { font-size: 1.75rem; }
    82Ôćĺh2 { font-size: 1.375rem; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); padding-bottom: 0.4rem; margin-top: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
    83Ôćĺh3 { font-size: 1.125rem; }
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺp { margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺul, ol { padding-left: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
    88Ôćĺli { margin-bottom: 0.25rem; }
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺtable { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 0.9rem; }
    91Ôćĺth, td { padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
    92Ôćĺth { color: var(--text-muted); font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.8rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; background: var(--bg-secondary); }
    93Ôćĺtr:hover td { background: var(--bg-secondary); }
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Nav ÔöÇÔöÇ */
    96Ôćĺ.nav {
    97Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-secondary);
    98Ôćĺ  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
    99Ôćĺ  padding: 0 1.5rem;
   100Ôćĺ  display: flex;
   101Ôćĺ  align-items: center;
   102Ôćĺ  gap: 0;
   103Ôćĺ  position: sticky;
   104Ôćĺ  top: 0;
   105Ôćĺ  z-index: 100;
   106Ôćĺ}
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ.nav-brand {
   109Ôćĺ  font-weight: 700;
   110Ôćĺ  font-size: 1rem;
   111Ôćĺ  color: var(--text);
   112Ôćĺ  padding: 0.75rem 1rem 0.75rem 0;
   113Ôćĺ  margin-right: 0.5rem;
   114Ôćĺ  border-right: 1px solid var(--border);
   115Ôćĺ}
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ.nav-link {
   118Ôćĺ  display: inline-block;
   119Ôćĺ  padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
   120Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   121Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.9rem;
   122Ôćĺ  transition: color 0.15s;
   123Ôćĺ}
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ.nav-link:hover { color: var(--text); text-decoration: none; }
   126Ôćĺ.nav-link.active { color: var(--text); border-bottom: 2px solid var(--accent); }
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Layout ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   129Ôćĺ.container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 1.5rem; }
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ.page-header { margin-bottom: 2rem; }
   132Ôćĺ.page-header h1 { font-size: 1.5rem; }
   133Ôćĺ.page-header .subtitle { color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem; margin-top: 0.25rem; }
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Cards ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   136Ôćĺ.card {
   137Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-card);
   138Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
   139Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
   140Ôćĺ  padding: 1.25rem;
   141Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1rem;
   142Ôćĺ}
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ.card-title {
   145Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.8rem;
   146Ôćĺ  font-weight: 600;
   147Ôćĺ  text-transform: uppercase;
   148Ôćĺ  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
   149Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   150Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
   151Ôćĺ}
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ.stat-value {
   154Ôćĺ  font-size: 2rem;
   155Ôćĺ  font-weight: 700;
   156Ôćĺ  color: var(--text);
   157Ôćĺ  line-height: 1;
   158Ôćĺ}
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ.stat-label { font-size: 0.85rem; color: var(--text-muted); margin-top: 0.25rem; }
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ.stats-grid {
   163Ôćĺ  display: grid;
   164Ôćĺ  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr));
   165Ôćĺ  gap: 1rem;
   166Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
   167Ôćĺ}
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Charts ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   170Ôćĺ.chart-grid {
   171Ôćĺ  display: grid;
   172Ôćĺ  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
   173Ôćĺ  gap: 1rem;
   174Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
   175Ôćĺ}
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ@media (max-width: 900px) {
   178Ôćĺ  .chart-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
   179Ôćĺ}
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ.chart-card {
   182Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-card);
   183Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
   184Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
   185Ôćĺ  padding: 1.25rem;
   186Ôćĺ}
   187Ôćĺ
   188Ôćĺ.chart-title {
   189Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.85rem;
   190Ôćĺ  font-weight: 600;
   191Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   192Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1rem;
   193Ôćĺ}
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ.chart-card svg { display: block; width: 100%; overflow: visible; }
   196Ôćĺ.chart-card.full-width { grid-column: 1 / -1; }
   197Ôćĺ
   198Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Badge ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   199Ôćĺ.badge {
   200Ôćĺ  display: inline-block;
   201Ôćĺ  padding: 0.2em 0.6em;
   202Ôćĺ  border-radius: 12px;
   203Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.75rem;
   204Ôćĺ  font-weight: 600;
   205Ôćĺ  text-transform: uppercase;
   206Ôćĺ  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
   207Ôćĺ}
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ.badge-codex { background: #1f6feb33; color: #58a6ff; border: 1px solid #1f6feb66; }
   210Ôćĺ.badge-claude { background: #6e40c933; color: #bc8cff; border: 1px solid #6e40c966; }
   211Ôćĺ.badge-gemini { background: #1a7f3733; color: #3fb950; border: 1px solid #1a7f3766; }
   212Ôćĺ
   213Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Messages ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   214Ôćĺ.messages { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; margin-top: 1rem; }
   215Ôćĺ
   216Ôćĺ.message {
   217Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
   218Ôćĺ  padding: 1rem;
   219Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid;
   220Ôćĺ  max-width: 100%;
   221Ôćĺ}
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ.message-user {
   224Ôćĺ  background: var(--user-bg);
   225Ôćĺ  border-color: var(--user-border);
   226Ôćĺ  align-self: flex-start;
   227Ôćĺ}
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ.message-assistant {
   230Ôćĺ  background: var(--assistant-bg);
   231Ôćĺ  border-color: var(--assistant-border);
   232Ôćĺ}
   233Ôćĺ
   234Ôćĺ.message-header {
   235Ôćĺ  display: flex;
   236Ôćĺ  align-items: center;
   237Ôćĺ  gap: 0.5rem;
   238Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
   239Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.8rem;
   240Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   241Ôćĺ}
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ.message-role {
   244Ôćĺ  font-weight: 600;
   245Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.8rem;
   246Ôćĺ  text-transform: capitalize;
   247Ôćĺ}
   248Ôćĺ
   249Ôćĺ.message-user .message-role { color: #58a6ff; }
   250Ôćĺ.message-assistant .message-role { color: #8b949e; }
   251Ôćĺ
   252Ôćĺ.message-body { font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.7; word-break: break-word; }
   253Ôćĺ.message-body p { margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
   254Ôćĺ.message-body p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
   255Ôćĺ
   256Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Summary card ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   257Ôćĺ.summary-card {
   258Ôćĺ  background: #161b22;
   259Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid #30363d;
   260Ôćĺ  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent);
   261Ôćĺ  border-radius: 6px;
   262Ôćĺ  padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
   263Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
   264Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.9rem;
   265Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   266Ôćĺ}
   267Ôćĺ
   268Ôćĺ.summary-card .summary-label {
   269Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.75rem;
   270Ôćĺ  font-weight: 600;
   271Ôćĺ  text-transform: uppercase;
   272Ôćĺ  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
   273Ôćĺ  color: var(--accent);
   274Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
   275Ôćĺ}
   276Ôćĺ
   277Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Metadata table ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   278Ôćĺ.meta-table { font-size: 0.85rem; margin-bottom: 1.25rem; }
   279Ôćĺ.meta-table td:first-child { color: var(--text-muted); width: 140px; font-weight: 500; }
   280Ôćĺ
   281Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Backlinks ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   282Ôćĺ.backlinks {
   283Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-secondary);
   284Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
   285Ôćĺ  border-radius: 6px;
   286Ôćĺ  padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
   287Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
   288Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.85rem;
   289Ôćĺ}
   290Ôćĺ
   291Ôćĺ.backlinks-title { font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-muted); margin-bottom: 0.4rem; font-size: 0.8rem; text-transform: uppercase; }
   292Ôćĺ.backlinks ul { padding-left: 1rem; }
   293Ôćĺ
   294Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Search ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   295Ôćĺ.search-box {
   296Ôćĺ  width: 100%;
   297Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-card);
   298Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
   299Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
   300Ôćĺ  padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
   301Ôćĺ  color: var(--text);
   302Ôćĺ  font-size: 1rem;
   303Ôćĺ  font-family: inherit;
   304Ôćĺ  margin-bottom: 1rem;
   305Ôćĺ  outline: none;
   306Ôćĺ  transition: border-color 0.15s;
   307Ôćĺ}
   308Ôćĺ
   309Ôćĺ.search-box:focus { border-color: var(--accent); }
   310Ôćĺ
   311Ôćĺ.search-results { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; }
   312Ôćĺ
   313Ôćĺ.search-result {
   314Ôćĺ  background: var(--bg-card);
   315Ôćĺ  border: 1px solid var(--border);
   316Ôćĺ  border-radius: 8px;
   317Ôćĺ  padding: 1rem;
   318Ôćĺ  transition: border-color 0.15s;
   319Ôćĺ}
   320Ôćĺ
   321Ôćĺ.search-result:hover { border-color: var(--accent); }
   322Ôćĺ.search-result-title { font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; }
   323Ôćĺ.search-result-meta { font-size: 0.8rem; color: var(--text-muted); margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
   324Ôćĺ.search-result-snippet { font-size: 0.85rem; color: var(--text-muted); }
   325Ôćĺ.search-result mark { background: #3d3000; color: var(--warning); border-radius: 2px; padding: 0 0.1em; }
   326Ôćĺ#search-count { font-size: 0.85rem; color: var(--text-muted); margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
   327Ôćĺ
   328Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Heatmap legend ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   329Ôćĺ.heatmap-legend {
   330Ôćĺ  display: flex;
   331Ôćĺ  align-items: center;
   332Ôćĺ  gap: 0.4rem;
   333Ôćĺ  font-size: 0.75rem;
   334Ôćĺ  color: var(--text-muted);
   335Ôćĺ  margin-top: 0.5rem;
   336Ôćĺ  justify-content: flex-end;
   337Ôćĺ}
   338Ôćĺ
   339Ôćĺ/* ÔöÇÔöÇ Responsive ÔöÇÔöÇ */
   340Ôćĺ@media (max-width: 600px) {
   341Ôćĺ  .nav { padding: 0 0.75rem; }
   342Ôćĺ  .container { padding: 1rem 0.75rem; }
   343Ôćĺ  .stats-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
   344Ôćĺ}
   345Ôćĺ`
   346Ôćĺ

| Issue | Fix |

Show output payload 
1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`.
     3Ôćĺ */
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺimport { createServer } from 'node:http'
     6Ôćĺimport { createReadStream, existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7Ôćĺimport { extname, join, resolve } from 'node:path'
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10Ôćĺ// Build command
    11Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺexport interface CmdReportBuildOptions {
    14Ôćĺ  root: string
    15Ôćĺ  output: string
    16Ôćĺ  noAi: boolean
    17Ôćĺ  model: string
    18Ôćĺ}
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺexport async function cmdReportBuild(opts: CmdReportBuildOptions): Promise<number> {
    21Ôćĺ  const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
    22Ôćĺ  try {
    23Ôćĺ    await buildReport({
    24Ôćĺ      root: resolve(opts.root),
    25Ôćĺ      output: resolve(opts.output),
    26Ôćĺ      noAi: opts.noAi,
    27Ôćĺ      model: opts.model,
    28Ôćĺ    })
    29Ôćĺ    console.log(`Report generated at: ${resolve(opts.output)}`)
    30Ôćĺ    console.log(`Open: ${join(resolve(opts.output), 'index.html')}`)
    31Ôćĺ    return 0
    32Ôćĺ  } catch (err: unknown) {
    33Ôćĺ    console.error(`Report build failed: ${String(err)}`)
    34Ôćĺ    return 1
    35Ôćĺ  }
    36Ôćĺ}
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39Ôćĺ// Serve command
    40Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺexport interface CmdReportServeOptions {
    43Ôćĺ  dir: string
    44Ôćĺ  port: number
    45Ôćĺ}
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺexport function cmdReportServe(opts: CmdReportServeOptions): number {
    48Ôćĺ  const root = resolve(opts.dir)
    49Ôćĺ  const port = opts.port
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ  const MIME: Record<string, string> = {
    52Ôćĺ    '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
    53Ôćĺ    '.css': 'text/css',
    54Ôćĺ    '.js': 'application/javascript',
    55Ôćĺ    '.json': 'application/json',
    56Ôćĺ    '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
    57Ôćĺ    '.ico': 'image/x-icon',
    58Ôćĺ  }
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ  const server = createServer((req, res) => {
    61Ôćĺ    let urlPath: string
    62Ôćĺ    try {
    63Ôćĺ      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')
    64Ôćĺ    } catch {
    65Ôćĺ      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    66Ôćĺ      res.end('Bad request')
    67Ôćĺ      return
    68Ôćĺ    }
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root
    73Ôćĺ    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + '/') && filePath !== root) {
    74Ôćĺ      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    75Ôćĺ      res.end('Forbidden')
    76Ôćĺ      return
    77Ôćĺ    }
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ    // Serve index.html for directory requests
    80Ôćĺ    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {
    81Ôćĺ      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')
    82Ôćĺ    }
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
    85Ôćĺ      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    86Ôćĺ      res.end('Not found')
    87Ôćĺ      return
    88Ôćĺ    }
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ    const ext = extname(filePath)
    91Ôćĺ    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'
    92Ôćĺ    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })
    93Ôćĺ    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)
    94Ôćĺ    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })
    95Ôćĺ    stream.pipe(res)
    96Ôćĺ  })
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ  server.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
    99Ôćĺ    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   100Ôćĺ      console.error(`Error: port ${port} is already in use.`)
   101Ôćĺ      process.exit(1)
   102Ôćĺ    }
   103Ôćĺ    console.error(`Server error: ${String(err)}`)
   104Ôćĺ    process.exit(1)
   105Ôćĺ  })
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ  server.listen(port, () => {
   108Ôćĺ    console.log(`Serving ${root}`)
   109Ôćĺ    console.log(`Open: http://localhost:${port}/`)
   110Ôćĺ    console.log('Press Ctrl+C to stop.')
   111Ôćĺ  })
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ  return 0
   114Ôćĺ}
   115Ôćĺ

assistant #334 10:37:25

Found it. On Windows, path.join produces backslashes (\) but the containment check uses root + '/' ÔÇö so every request fails because D:\...\07_reports\index.html doesn't start with D:\...\07_reports/. The slash vs backslash mismatch triggers 403 for every path.

{
  "command": "cd \"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\" && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(report): add memorytree report build & serve commands\n\nGenerates a self-contained multi-page HTML memory website from\ntranscripts, goals, and knowledge files, with inline SVG charts\n(activity heatmap, client doughnut, weekly line, top-tools bar),\nfull-text search with embedded JSON index, optional per-session AI\nsummaries cached by raw_sha256, and heartbeat daemon integration.\n\nNew files:\n- src/types/report.ts              ÔÇö ReportStats, BuildReportOptions, etc.\n- src/report/stats.ts              ÔÇö pure statistics + isoWeekKey()\n- src/report/summarize.ts          ÔÇö AI summary with sha256 cache (max-5 semaphore)\n- src/report/build.ts              ÔÇö orchestrator; writes Memory/07_reports/\n- src/report/render/css.ts         ÔÇö GitHub Dark self-contained CSS\n- src/report/render/layout.ts      ÔÇö escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, clientBadge, slugifyName\n- src/report/render/charts.ts      ÔÇö all inline SVG charts\n- src/report/render/dashboard.ts   ÔÇö stat cards + charts + recent sessions\n- src/report/render/transcript-list.ts\n- src/report/render/transcript.ts\n- src/report/render/knowledge.ts\n- src/report/render/goals.ts\n- src/report/render/markdown.ts    ÔÇö minimal MarkdownÔćĺHTML (no external lib)\n- src/report/render/links.ts       ÔÇö backlink graph from [[id]] refs\n- src/report/render/search.ts      ÔÇö full-text search page (inline JS index)\n- src/cli/cmd-report.ts            ÔÇö build + serve CLI adapters\n- tests/report/**                  ÔÇö 67 new tests (484 total, 0 failing)\n\nModified:\n- src/cli/main.ts          ÔÇö register 'report build' and 'report serve'\n- src/heartbeat/config.ts  ÔÇö add generate_report, ai_summary_model fields\n- src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts ÔÇö auto-generate report after git push\n- tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts ÔÇö cover new config fields\n- package.json             ÔÇö add @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency\n\nSecurity fixes: escHtml on all user content; </script> escape in JSON\nindex; path.sep containment check in serve (fixes Windows Forbidden bug);\nCSS class allowlist in clientBadge; cache stored outside gitignored dir.\nEOF\n)\"",

     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Key Project

     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Architecture
     8Ôćĺ- NodeNext ESM TypeScript, strict mode + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` + `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`
     9Ôćĺ- All imports need `.js` extension (e.g., `from './stats.js'`)
    10Ôćĺ- Tests in `tests/` with Vitest, mock `node:os` homedir to tmpdir
    11Ôćĺ- Build with `tsup`, entry: `src/cli/main.ts` Ôćĺ `dist/cli.js`
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ## Report Module (implemented 2026-03-16)
    14Ôćĺ- `src/types/report.ts` ÔÇö ReportStats, SummaryCache, LinkGraph, SearchIndexEntry, BuildReportOptions
    15Ôćĺ- `src/report/build.ts` ÔÇö orchestrator: buildReport(), parseMessagesFromMarkdown(), ensureGitignore()
    16Ôćĺ- `src/report/stats.ts` ÔÇö computeStats(), buildDayBuckets(), extractToolNames()
    17Ôćĺ- `src/report/summarize.ts` ÔÇö getSummary() with sha256 cache, semaphore (max 5), dynamic @anthropic-ai/sdk
    18Ôćĺ- `src/report/render/` ÔÇö css, layout, charts (SVG only), dashboard, transcript-list, transcript, knowledge, goals, search, links, markdown
    19Ôćĺ- Output: `Memory/07_reports/` (.gitignored), never committed
    20Ôćĺ- CLI: `memorytree report build --root . --no-ai` and `memorytree report serve`
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ## Config Fields Added
    23Ôćĺ- `generate_report: boolean` (default: false) ÔÇö trigger report after heartbeat
    24Ôćĺ- `ai_summary_model: string` (default: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ## Clean Markdown Format
    27ÔćĺMessages in `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/{client}/.../*.md`:
    28Ôćĺ- Section: `## Messages`
    29Ôćĺ- Per message: `### N. role\n- Timestamp: \`ISO\`\n\ntext`
    30Ôćĺ- No tool events in clean markdown (only in raw JSONL)
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ## Test Count
    33Ôćĺ- 484 tests passing (was 401)
    34Ôćĺ- New tests: stats, charts, links, search, summarize, build (integration), config new fields

     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺimport { parse as parseToml } from 'smol-toml'
    11Ôćĺ

    20Ôćĺconst DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL = 'info'
    21Ôćĺconst DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT = false
    22Ôćĺconst DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
    23Ôćĺconst VALID_LOG_LEVELS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'])
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26Ôćĺ// Types
    27Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    29Ôćĺexport interface ProjectEntry {

    32Ôćĺ}
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺexport interface Config {

    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺexport function configPath(): string {
    53Ôćĺ  return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'config.toml')

    56Ôćĺ// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺexport function loadConfig(): Config {
    61Ôćĺ  const path = configPath()
    62Ôćĺ  if (!existsSync(path)) {
    63Ôćĺ    return defaultConfig()
    64Ôćĺ  }

    66Ôćĺ  try {

    69Ôćĺ  } catch {
    70Ôćĺ    return defaultConfig()
    71Ôćĺ  }
    72Ôćĺ  return parseRaw(raw)
    73Ôćĺ}
    74Ôćĺ

Tool Result #393 WebFetch toolu_019wHzXJz96FC6JtHE5xW95L 11:16:03
WebFetch output=# Major Features of Quartz 4 Based on the official documentation, Quartz includes these notable capabilities: **Core Features:** - "Obsidian compatibility, full-text search, gra...
Show output payload 
# Major Features of Quartz 4

Based on the official documentation, Quartz includes these notable capabilities:

**Core Features:**
- "Obsidian compatibility, full-text search, graph view, wikilinks, transclusions, backlinks, Latex, syntax highlighting, popover previews, Docker Support, internationalization, comments" and additional functionality

**Performance & Development:**
- Hot-reload during configuration changes with incremental content rebuilds
- "Ridiculously fast page loads and tiny bundle sizes"
- Simple JSX-based layouts with custom page components

**Customization:**
- Fully-customizable parsing and page generation via plugins
- Theme flexibility for personalization
- Mobile-responsive design support

**Content Management:**
- Wiki-style link system for interconnected notes
- Visual graph representation of content connections
- Embedded content through transclusions
- Mathematical notation support via LaTeX

**User Experience:**
- Preview tooltips when hovering over links
- Dark and light mode options
- Comprehensive full-text search functionality
- Table of contents generation
- Reading time estimates

**Distribution:**
- RSS feed generation
- Sitemap creation for SEO
- Static site hosting compatibility

Quartz is designed to transform Markdown into fully functional websites, particularly suited for personal knowledge bases, digital gardens, and note-sharing platforms.
Tool Result #394 WebFetch toolu_01CzJn1kbM8KVqzWiqQCQ4mv 11:16:06
WebFetch output=# Quartz Features Based on the provided GitHub repository content, specific feature lists, plugins, and detailed capabilities are **not included** in the webpage excerpt shown. ...
Show output payload 
# Quartz Features

Based on the provided GitHub repository content, specific feature lists, plugins, and detailed capabilities are **not included** in the webpage excerpt shown.

The page only identifies Quartz as:

> "a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites"

**What we know:**
- It's designed for publishing digital gardens and notes as websites
- It's free to use
- The codebase is primarily TypeScript (85.2%)
- It includes SCSS, JavaScript, and CSS components
- Full documentation is available at quartz.jzhao.xyz

To find the comprehensive feature list, plugin system details, and specific capabilities, you would need to visit the official documentation site referenced in the README rather than rely on this GitHub overview page.

I need the complete feature list including: graph view details, search implementation, backlinks, table of contents, popover previews, tags, breadcrumbs, recent notes, RSS, sitemap, syntax highlighting, LaTeX, transclusions, reading time, folder pages, tag pages, 404 page, aliases, dark/light mode toggle.",
